races.race_name: Oliver Brown Pretty Polly Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Esna
runners.spotlight: All three starts as 2yo were on soft; green in front in 7f novice win at Sandown against a notable rival; about 2l fourth of eight when an outsider for 1m Group 1 Marcel Boussac at Longchamp, keeping on well while never able to threaten seriously the lead; fair chance she'll stay 1m2f, judged on that, even if pedigree (sire sprinter) casts doubts on her Oaks entry; clear top on form.
runners.comment: Clear top on form after her Group 1 Marcel Boussac fourth; fair chance she'll stay 1m2f

runners.name: Jennifer Jane
runners.spotlight: Made all in a maiden at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on second start and almost all in Listed race at Sandown (7f, good) later last summer; her season ended, however, with last of seven in Group 3 at Goodwood (7f, good to firm) in August; good chance she will stay 1m2f; needs to resume progress.
runners.comment: Good chance she will stay 1m2f; needs to resume progress after last in a Group 3 in August

runners.name: Sacred Ground
runners.spotlight: By Kingman out of Oaks winner Anapurna; favourite for both starts as 2yo, overcoming greenness to win a five-runner novice at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) and every chance through the final 3f when second of ten in Listed race at Newmarket (1m, good to soft); Oaks entry from a stable which has a fine record in this race and houses the current Oaks favourite, so market strength would probably be telling.
runners.comment: By Kingman out of Oaks winner Anapurna; won debut and second of ten in 1m Listed race here

runners.name: Lilt
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega half-sister to winners Doom (7f/1m including Listed; RPR 104) and Kilt (1m; 97); dam a well-related 1m-1m2f winner (including US Grade 1; 116); 7-2 for a six-runner maiden at Doncaster (7f, soft) in October, dictating a steady pace and battling on well to win by a neck; that's just fair form but this 1m Coronation Stakes and 1m4f Irish Oaks entry looks the type to do a lot better as 3yo.
runners.comment: 7f Doncaster maiden win as 2yo is just fair form but she looks the type to do a lot better

runners.name: Brigid's Well
runners.spotlight: Made all in one 1m novice as a 2yo and very nearly did so in another; 11-1 fourth of ten in Listed race here (1m, good to soft; Sacred Ground was second) on final start illustrated some limitations, sticking to her task well as if this longer trip would suit but, despite the Oaks entry, she probably has serious work to do in this company too.
runners.comment: 11-1 fourth of ten in 1m Listed race here on final 2yo start, with Sacred Ground in second

runners.name: Spinning Lizzie
runners.spotlight: Won debut as 2yo but twice came up short in Group events; resumed with a late charge to grab a conditions stakes at Kempton (1m, AW) in March but the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good; 22-1, made most) proved too tough and, despite a new trip, the same is likely to be the case today.
runners.comment: Has come up short in her three Group events and the same is likely to be the case today

runners.name: Maldives
runners.spotlight: 2,900,000gns yearling by Camelot; first foal of Group-placed 7f 2yo winner (RPR 102), herself a half-sister to multiple Group 1 winner Luxembourg; 8-1 in steadily run novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) three weeks ago, short of room for a sustained period before running on into second of seven; Irish Oaks entry who is bottom on ratings but probably capable of a lot better.
runners.comment: 2,900,000gns yearling by Camelot; hampered when second in novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good)

runners.name: Survie
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare who has several pieces of Group 1 form over middle distances, including a Saudi effort since joining new yard this year; unsuited by drop back to 1m last time when one place behind Cathedral, losing her unbeaten AW record; big player on peak figures.
runners.comment: Has several pieces of Group 1 form, including Saudi effort for new yard; big player

runners.name: Cathedral
runners.spotlight: Possibly unsuited by soft ground; record of 0-8 since debut success doesn't do her justice, with three good efforts at Group/Grade 1 level (1m/1m3f) among her form; leading player on ratings returned to turf with AW reappearance under her belt.
runners.comment: Form includes three good efforts at Group/Grade 1 level; leading player on ratings

runners.name: Stateira
runners.spotlight: Doing well on AW and justified favouritism in 7f handicap on finals day, taking Newcastle record to 3-3; far from certain to sustain her progress over this longer trip back on turf (second attempt in this sphere).
runners.comment: Doing well on AW but is far from certain to sustain her progress back on turf

runners.name: Cheshire Dancer
runners.spotlight: Developed into a very useful sort last term, notably Group/Grade 3 performances at Ascot (won) and Kentucky Downs (ran well); chance of landing this prize depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of landing this prize depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: American Gal
runners.spotlight: Steady progress culminated in close second to Cheshire Dancer in Group 3 at Ascot (1m) on King George day last year; not in the same form since and this is a stiff task even if she rebounds.
runners.comment: Not in the same form since close second in Ascot Group 3 on King George day

runners.name: Sand Gazelle
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly; below form at Newmarket when last seen but broadly progressive otherwise and may have more to offer this term; Listed winner at York (1m2f) last July then ran well in Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f); shortlisted.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly; broadly progressive and may have more to offer this term

runners.name: Miss Justice
runners.spotlight: Form for the Gosdens featured a Listed win last August and close second in Newmarket Group 3 in October (both 1m2f); sold for 750,000gns since; this is harder but she may do better still.
runners.comment: Close second at Newmarket on final start for the Gosdens; sold 750,000gns since

runners.name: Arisaig
runners.spotlight: Ran well off top weight in handicap at the Newmarket Craven meeting (1m, good) but has something to find on ratings off level weights in this higher grade; not disgraced in Goodwood Listed event on Saturday.
runners.comment: Ran well in handicap at the Craven meeting; not disgraced at Goodwood on Saturday

runners.name: Falakeyah
runners.spotlight: Has proved headstrong and she was disappointing in last two appearances, including when only 5-2 for the Coronation Stakes having bypassed the Oaks and French Oaks, but this may be the time to catch her; comfortably justified favouritism in the Pretty Polly on this card (good to firm) 12 months ago, taking record to 2-2 and looking a potentially high-class filly; worth another chance.
runners.comment: Looks worth another chance to confirm the high promise of her win on this card last year

runners.name: Chantilly Lace
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; good fifth in the 1,000 Guineas and Coronation Stakes prior to close third in Ascot Group 3 won by Cheshire Dancer; below par in final 3yo start but consistent otherwise and may rebound.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; consistent until below par on last appearance; may rebound

runners.name: Francophone
runners.spotlight: Successful twice in Listed grade, including at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on last appearance; could go well but her overall record and ratings suggest she's unlikely to follow up in this better race.
runners.comment: Listed win at Newmarket on last appearance; unlikely to follow up in this grade

runners.name: Jancis
runners.spotlight: Irish mare whose career commenced with two Leopardstown wins, including in Group 3; 0-7 since but has continued to show useful form; unlikely to get back on the scoresheet in this field, however.
runners.comment: Useful sort but looks unlikely to regain the winning thread in this field

runners.name: Bahadur
runners.spotlight: Made immediate progress when switched to AW in the autumn, completing a hat-trick in a 1m4f novice and 1m4f/1m6f handicaps; perhaps in need of the race (albeit joint-favourite) on reappearance four weeks ago, but now needs to show that he can serve up his best on turf.
runners.comment: Made immediate progress when switched to AW in the autumn; needs to show show same on turf
runners.owner: J William Rowley & S P C Woods

runners.name: Goblet Of Fire
runners.spotlight: All of his Flat success for this yard has been on AW, albeit with good-ground hurdle wins too and he was runner-up at Goodwood (1m6f, good to firm) last August; has a run under his belt this term but to win this would require a new best performance on turf.
runners.comment: Has a run under his belt but to win this would require a new best performance on turf

runners.name: Dramatic Star
runners.spotlight: Improved last year without quite winning; each-way claims if back to best with first-time headgear after two rather disappointing runs in 2026.
runners.comment: E-w claims if back to best with first-time headgear after two rather disappointing runs

runners.name: Charging Thunder
runners.spotlight: Had underwhelming hurdle and AW campaigns before his second of five when returned to Flat turf at Beverley (2m) 18 days ago; this demands markedly better and his 2024 and 2025 turf records aren't that persuasive.
runners.comment: 2nd of 5 when returned to Flat turf at Beverley (2m) 18 days ago; this demands much more

runners.name: Yashin
runners.spotlight: Started 2025 with a bang for new connections when winning a slowly run, four-runner Group 3 at Ascot (2m, good to firm) in April; remote with ambitious targets on next three starts and although sixth of 11 in 2m handicap at Chester in September was an upturn, markedly better is required to win this..
runners.comment: Started 2025 with a bang (won slowly run Group 3) but there was only a whimper afterwards

runners.name: Brasil Power
runners.spotlight: Moving up to 1m4f worked well in December and comparable form at 1m6f/2m since, including another win three starts back; he's come up a bit short in warmer races this year, though, including behind Align The Stars at Kempton, and none of his turf form (admittedly at up to 1m2f) is close to this mark.
runners.comment: This race perhaps too tough; no turf form (admittedly at up to 1m2f) is close to this mark

runners.name: Pole Star
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term and two respectable efforts in valuable races in late summer; just 10-1 for 2m2f Cesarewitch at Newmarket on final 3yo outing, which did not work out, and he was just 15-8 when fading into fourth of six at Newbury (2m) 16 days ago on first run since he was gelded; connections now turn to a visor and drop him back in trip.
runners.comment: Just 15-8 when fading into 4th of six at Newbury (2m); visor and back down in trip
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Artisan Dancer
runners.spotlight: Industrious and dependable sort over 1m6f-2m2f, who's fit from AW, but his turf strike-rate is just 1-17 and that makes him look vulnerable.
runners.comment: Industrious and dependable sort over 1m6f-2m2f but his turf strike-rate is just 1-17

runners.name: Many Men
runners.spotlight: Excellent progress last term included three handicap wins (1m2f, 1m6f and 2m) in March-July and an agonisingly close call in the Melrose at York in August; close fourth in 1m6f Listed race at Ascot on final outing; well below that form on 2026 reappearance but that may have put him right.
runners.comment: Excellent progress as 3yo; well below best on 2026 return but that may have put him right
runners.owner: J Finch, Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

runners.name: Subsequent
runners.spotlight: Excellent 3yo season culminated in his Listed win at Ascot (1m6f, soft) and, after a delayed reappearance last year, he found career-best form to win a good handicap at Doncaster (14.5f, good to soft; tongue tied second time) in September on final outing; the Yorkshire Cup and Ascot Gold Cup entries are striking but ideally he would be on ground softer than good.
runners.comment: Yorkshire Cup and Ascot Gold Cup entries are striking but ground softer than good is ideal

runners.name: Align The Stars
runners.spotlight: Thereabouts on occasions in 2025, crept down the weights and broke his losing run in clearcut style when he reappeared five weeks ago wearing restored cheekpieces in the Queen's Prize at Kempton (2m, AW); back up 4lb but showed plenty of bottle in that win and may have turned a corner.
runners.comment: May have turned a corner when he broke his losing run in clearcut style at Kempton (AW)

runners.name: Kihavah
runners.spotlight: Saw daylight all too late when strong-finishing fifth in this race last year (much better than his 2024 performance in it); Meydan this January/February did not yield much (stiff tasks) but he's now 4lb lower than this time last year, too well handicapped to be ruled out; rare run in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Saw daylight all too late when strong-finishing 5th in this race last year off 4lb higher

runners.name: Precise
runners.spotlight: Excellent progress last term culminated in Group 1 wins at the Curragh (7f, yielding) and Newmarket, bringing up a four-timer; drew clear inside the final 1f and comfortably beat Venetian Lace by just over 3l in the Fillies' Mile over C&D (good) on the final occasion; had a minor setback to her schedule with a slightly raised temperature in March but is apparently fine now; trainer has landed this race seven times; leading contender.
runners.comment: Continued her excellent progress with comfortable win in the Fillies' Mile; leading player

runners.name: My Highness
runners.spotlight: Ended her 2yo campaign with quite a tidy success in Group 2 at Deauville (7f, good) to complete a hat-trick; beaten favourite (finished second, splitting outsiders) in the Prix Imprudence over the same C&D on reappearance but that was also the case with the same yard's Miss France, who rebounded to land this prize in 2014; likely to be suited by stepping up to 1m; enters calculations, with a resumption of improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Similar profile to the same yard's winner of this race in 2014; may well resume progress

runners.name: True Love
runners.spotlight: In the same ball park as stablemate Precise on ratings and, like that filly, is a Group 1 winner at Newmarket (Cheveley Park Stakes over 6f); landed the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot (5f) earlier in her 2yo campaign and added a Group 3 prize, with something to spare, at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; has a solid record aside from an excusable defeat at the Breeders' Cup; upped further in trip and has stamina on the dam's side; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Cheveley Park Stakes winner; has a largely solid record; one of the main contenders
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Venetian Sun
runners.spotlight: Absent since finishing third to Precise in the Moyglare Stud Stakes when upped to 7f at the Curragh, losing her unbeaten record but far from disgraced and sticking on in a manner that suggests she'll stay 1m this year; 4-4 previously and completed a notable 6f hat-trick with wins in Group 3 at Royal Ascot, Group 2 at Newmarket (July) and Group 1 at Deauville; may well rate higher still and add to an already impressive tally; pleased her trainer in a racecourse gallop last month; highly respected.
runners.comment: Below-form 3rd to Precise on final 2yo effort but 4-4 previously; may well resume progress

runners.name: Evolutionist
runners.spotlight: Newmarket form comprises a maiden win on the July course and third-place finish, just over 4l behind Precise, in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile; bagged Group 3 honours at Longchamp (1m, good) on reappearance, collaring a sitting duck (clear leader who tired) and strong at the finish; has evidently trained on well and her RPRs continue on an upward trajectory.
runners.comment: Strong at the finish in Group 3 at Longchamp, showing that she has trained on well

runners.name: Azleet
runners.spotlight: Belied her 50-1 odds with neck success in the Nell Gwyn (upped to 7f, good; Group 3) on seasonal debut, taking strike-rate to 3-8 and form figures on the Newmarket tracks to 121; doesn't look a budding star and only two fillies in the last 40 years have landed that trial then followed up in the 1,000 Guineas; may well come up short.
runners.comment: Won the Nell Gwyn but doesn't look a budding star and may well come up short

runners.name: Abashiri
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly, by Frankel out of a 1m2f Group 1 scorer for her connections; quickened smartly and recorded an impressive 4l success in Kempton maiden (1m, AW) six months ago; the shortest on experience, being the sole once-raced contender, but brings major potential; interesting.
runners.comment: Well bred; quickened smartly for an impressive win at Kempton; brings major potential

runners.name: Mubasimah
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly who has been campaigned exclusively over 7f at Newmarket, producing good performances (won maiden and second in Group 3) from the front last autumn and then not asked to lead when only sixth, beaten favourite, in the Nell Gwyn; may still have more to offer but this is a difficult task in terms of form and tactical considerations.
runners.comment: Only sixth when favourite for the Nell Gwyn; low in calculations after that effort

runners.name: Timeforshowcasing
runners.spotlight: Held on gamely in 1m Listed event at Newcastle on seasonal debut, taking AW record to 2-2 and overall strike-rate to 4-6; may win more races at the right level but looks out of her depth in this company returned to turf, as her two defeats (both Group 2) attest.
runners.comment: Has built up a good-strike rate but looks out of her depth at this level

runners.name: Darn Hot Gallop
runners.spotlight: From the family of Enable; edged ahead close home in C&D handicap (good; ran off a mark of 83) at the Craven meeting, adding to her 2yo AW wins; it's hard to knock an improving filly who remains unbeaten (3-3) but her form is a long way short of Classic standard, as BHA ratings reflect.
runners.comment: Won C&D handicap at the Craven meeting, taking record to 3-3; this is much harder

runners.name: Inis Mor
runners.spotlight: Solid 2yo form with two novice wins on the Newmarket July course and a good second in valuable conditions race at Longchamp (sole 1m attempt); didn't particularly boost her Guineas claims with 2l fourth to Asleet in the Nell Gwyn (7f, good) but she may take a step forward with that reappearance outing under her belt.
runners.comment: Solid 2yo form; may take a step forward with Nell Gwyn reappearance under her belt

runners.name: Touleen
runners.spotlight: Gained her 2yo wins in stylish fashion and made a satisfactory reappearance as favourite for the Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) when a close second under Saffie Osborne; bit to prove back at Newmarket, having looked ill at ease here in the Rockfel, but a plausible danger provided she handles the track better this time; from the family of 1,000 Guineas winner Ghanaati and retains potential.
runners.comment: Satisfactory reappearance in the Fred Darling; from the family of a 1,000 Guineas winner

runners.name: The Prettiest Star
runners.spotlight: Daughter of her connections' July Cup winner Starman; duly showed plenty of promise in both 2yo starts, recording a clearcut success at Nottingham (6f, good) and close second, clear of remainder, in the Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm; Group 2); gives the impression she'll be suited by this further step up in distance; possibilities with additional improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Clear second in the Rockfel; possibilities with further improvement on the cards

runners.name: Domina Ignis
runners.spotlight: Very similar profile to the 2024 Guineas winner Elmalka, having won at Southwell in sole 2yo start then finished third in the Fred Darling (Group 3); gained her success (6f, AW) in emphatic fashion despite taking a good hold, then was beaten only 2l with hood fitted at Newbury (7f, good) despite being hampered; bred to stay 1m and looks open to further progress.
runners.comment: Good third in the Fred Darling; very similar profile to Elmalka, who won this race in 2024

runners.name: Spicy Marg
runners.spotlight: Proved a useful sprinter last season, notably winning over 5f at Glorious Goodwood in the summer and over 6f at Newmarket (beat Azleet in good-ground Listed contest) final outing; not an obvious 1,000 Guineas player, with doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters (she's a stone below the standard required).
runners.comment: Useful sprinter last season; has doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters

runners.name: Silenciosa
runners.spotlight: Scored with something to spare at Southwell (AW) in sole 2yo start, then did well to finish sixth in the Fred Darling at Newbury (again over 7f) having been slowly away, met traffic issues and been forced to switch; remains a nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands another big leap forward on bare figures.
runners.comment: Nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands plenty of further progress

runners.name: Venetian Lace
runners.spotlight: Belied market position (rank outsider) by finishing second to Precise in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile over C&D but needs to prove she can back up that apparent improvement; may be flattered by her new rating and, combined with a record of 0-5 since debut success, doesn't look a solid contestant.
runners.comment: Needs to prove she can back up the form of her big-priced second in the Fillies' Mile
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Rose Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Has something to find at this level but is open to further improvement and would be particularly interesting if the ground is on the soft side, having shown her 2yo promise on slower than good, namely a narrow success in newcomers' event at Deauville (7f) and a rallying fourth in the May Hill at Doncaster (1m Group 2).
runners.comment: Showed her 2yo promise on softer than good; interesting if the ground is similar

runners.name: True Test
runners.spotlight: Career commenced with two wins, the second one on the Newmarket July course; record of 0-5 since reflects that her limitations are rather exposed, and this is a markedly stronger Guineas than the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas in which she went close at Meydan (1m, good; Listed) last time.
runners.comment: Went close in the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas at Meydan; this is a much stronger race

races.race_name: Tattersalls £40,000 EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Call Me Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Weak 9-1 shot for her C&D debut 18 days ago but she made a bold bid, travelling strongly but finding one too strong in the closing stages; that effort was backed up by the Topspeed figure and she sets a useful standard for the newcomers to aim at before factoring in progress.
runners.comment: Shaped well when second over C&D last month; leading claims with progress likely

runners.name: Lazurite
runners.spotlight: March foal by Blue Point; £175,000 yearling; closely related to 6f-1m winner Hallasan (including 2yo; RPR 99), half-sister to four winners including Al Raya (5f 2yo including Group 3; 105), Grisons (5f-6f; 79) and Mystify Me (5f; 70); dam 5f and 6f (Listed) 2yo winner (99); trainer has had a winning 2yo newcomer this year; William Buick booked.
runners.comment: £175,000 yearling with five winning siblings; dam a Listed winner; a newcomer of note

runners.name: Efsixteen
runners.spotlight: Late April foal by Havana Grey; 135,000gns yearling, 350,000gns breeze-up 2yo; eighth foal; sister to 5f/6f winner Pilgrim (including 2yo; RPR 106), half-sister to winners Moraawed (6f/7f; 92) and Crash Helmet (6f AW; 71); dam 5f 2yo winner (74), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Temple Meads; looks the part on paper and she makes her debut with her yard in form.
runners.comment: 350,000gns breeze-up 2yo; sharp pedigree and makes debut for an in-form trainer

runners.name: Crownbreaker
runners.spotlight: March foal; 550,000gns yearling; fourth foal; Minzaal half-sister to winners Mill Stream (6f including 2yo/Group 1 July Cup; RPR 120) and Asymmetric (5f/6f including 2yo Group 2; 109); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; 83); strong paper claims for a trainer who is already off the mark with his 2yos.
runners.comment: 550,000gns half-sister to July Cup winner Mill Stream; yard off mark with 2yos; contender

runners.name: Donna Beauty
runners.spotlight: March foal; 35,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Starspangledbanner half-sister to four winners including Grocer Jack (1m2f/10.5f including German/Italian Group 3; RPR 116) and Ghislaine (7f-8.7f including Listed; 99); dam German 1m-1m3f winner (including Group 3; 104); not obviously precocious on paper but worth a market check.
runners.comment: 35,000gns half-sister to 4 winners out of a useful German mare; not obviously precocious

runners.name: Havana Sprite
runners.spotlight: January foal; 150,000gns yearling; second foal; Havana Grey half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Polly Darling (RPR 71); dam 6f/6.5f 2yo winner (93); stable 0-2 with their 2yos this year but had one run well behind Call Me Tomorrow last month.
runners.comment: 150,000gns yearling; half-sister to a minor 5f 2yo winner; dam a winning 2yo; check market

runners.name: Holi Scarlett
runners.spotlight: April foal by Bungle Inthejungle; 17,000euros yearling; sixth foal; sister to 5f/6f winner Lily In The Jungle (including 2yo; RPR 74), half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Lili Wen Fach (69); dam 5f winner (71), out of half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 3 winner Show Me The Money; bred to have a future but she is starting out in a potentially hot contest.
runners.comment: 17,000euros yearling; bred to be sharp but could be starting out in a hot race

runners.name: Pageant Girl
runners.spotlight: Promise amid inexperience on her Beverley debut (5f, good to soft) and still looked a work in progress when winning a 6f novice at Ripon eight days ago; had the benefit of the rail to help her but she kept on well enough; she will need to progress again to defy a penalty against some interesting opposition, though.
runners.comment: Stepped up on Beverley debut when winning at Ripon (6f, good) 8 days ago; this is tougher

races.race_name: Tattersalls Online £40,000 EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jaan Ki Tukri
runners.spotlight: Promise amid inexperience when third of nine in a 5f maiden (good ground) at Newbury 16 days ago, racing freely early on and paying the price in the closing stages; finished a neck in front of Napa on that occasion and can confirm the placings.
runners.comment: Pleasing 3rd at Newbury on debut (green off the bridle; neck in front of Napa); contender

runners.name: The Dancing Pirate
runners.spotlight: May 28 foal by Blackbeard; third foal of a smart 6f/7f winner (including Group 3; RPR 106), herself from an excellent family; should have a future but comfortably the youngest of these and he will need to be useful to make a winning start.
runners.comment: Dam a smart 6f/7f winner; appeal on paper but he's a late foal for early-season 2yo races

runners.name: Agamemnon
runners.spotlight: February foal by Mehmas; £72,000 yearling, 150,000gns breeze-up 2yo; first foal of a once-raced 7f 2yo winner (RPR 89); trainer off the mark with 2yos this year; William Buick booked; market confidence would look significant.
runners.comment: 150,000gns breeze-up 2yo; dam once-raced 2yo winner; William Buick booked; check betting

runners.name: Napa
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite for his Newbury debut 16 days ago (5f, good) and ran creditably, not helped by being a bit tight for room inside the final furlong; finished a neck behind Jaan Ki Tukri on that occasion and he is no banker to reverse the placings.
runners.comment: Sent off favourite for his debut and ran creditably for 4th (a neck behind Jaan Ki Turki)

runners.name: Persian Spring
runners.spotlight: April foal; 100,000gns yearling by Persian Force; eighth foal; closely related to 6f 2yo winner Beauty Inspire (including Group 3; RPR 102), half-brother to winners Darwell Lion (7f-8.6f including 2yo/AW; 97) and Camera Shy (6f/7f including 2yo/AW; 78); dam French 1m4f winner, out of 5f/6f winner; represents a powerful yard; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 100,000gns yearling; three winning siblings (inc Group 3); powerful yard; shortlisted

runners.name: Beauty For Love
runners.spotlight: March foal by Blue Point; 140,000euros foal, 130,000euros yearling; sixth foal; closely related to Italian 7.5f-1m2f winner Conquering Eagle, half-brother to winners Apricot Ice (6f; RPR 95), Saraqael (1m; 93) and Seasony (7f/1m including 2yo/AW/Czechia; 79); dam Group-placed 1m winner (100); enough paper appeal to warrant a second look.
runners.comment: 130,000euros yearling with four winning siblings; dam a useful 1m winner; can't dismiss

runners.name: Noble Raider
runners.spotlight: Always on the back foot on his Wolverhampton debut 12 days ago (5f) and although he ran on for second, he was no threat to the easy winner; should have learned from that initial experience but connections look to hold stronger claims with Napa.
runners.comment: Pushed along throughout when never-dangerous 2nd at Wolverhampton 12 days ago; yard run 2

runners.name: Study Of Words
runners.spotlight: Marked improvement through three-race 2yo career, beating a fair maiden by 15l when he won a four-runner maiden at 1-7 over C&D (good) final start; that can't be the most solid form but he could hardly have done it more easily and is a Derby entry; fascinating handicap debutant in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Beat a fair maiden 15l to win four-runner maiden at 1-7 over C&D (good) on final 2yo start

runners.name: Gatehouse
runners.spotlight: All three runs were on AW, with plenty of promise on debut before he won a novice at Chelmsford (1m2f) in December and seven-runner handicap by a short head at Southwell (1m) in April; return to 1m2f should suit and, although he's been raised 4lb, further improvement is possible.
runners.comment: A 2nd and two wins on AW; return to 1m2f should suit and further improvement is possible

runners.name: Crockham Heath
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at 50-1 on debut here (7f, good) in October; way better, however, for front-running wins in a Southwell novice (1m) in November and seven-runner handicap at Wolverhampton (9.4f) in January; those wins were AW but with authority last time (8lb higher today) and he could be a useful prospect.
runners.comment: Well beaten here on debut; won twice on AW, with authority last time; could be useful

runners.name: Sahara King
runners.spotlight: 16-1 for novice at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in November but always prominent and led final strides; 14-1 in a hot novice at Newbury (1m2f, good) 16 days ago and made late gains to finish on heels of the leaders in a blanket finish; set for further improvement and likely to prove better than this opening mark at some point soon.
runners.comment: AW win on debut; late gains for close 5th in hot Newbury novice (1m2f, good) 16 days ago
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Evanesco
runners.spotlight: Pretty useful in two of his five efforts as 2yo; second favourite for handicap/seasonal debut over C&D (good) 18 days ago but weakened in the final 1f into fourth of eight; that was creditable but he needs to build on it.
runners.comment: Second favourite for handicap/seasonal debut over C&D but weakened into fourth of eight

races.race_name: Aspire Cleaning & Facilities Ltd Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Westport
runners.spotlight: 270,000gns yearling by Blue Point; his wins have been over 6f (AW/good) but dropping back to 5f shouldn't be remotely troublesome in this company and the Godolphin horse he beat last time had won a Newmarket maiden; carries all the penalties but impossible to oppose.
runners.comment: Carries all the penalties but impossible to oppose given the level reached already
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Sanny Doo
runners.spotlight: Finished last in one start for James Tate and suffered the same fate on debut for this yard at Doncaster when a long way behind You Mystify Me; the hood tried there is removed.
runners.comment: Has finished last in both his races, latterly well behind You Mystify Me

runners.name: You Mystify Me
runners.spotlight: Posted an RPR in the 50s when a remote third at Doncaster (5f, good to firm); looks good for second spot but unlikely to be troubling the favourite.
runners.comment: Posted an RPR in the 50s when a remote third at Doncaster (5f, good to firm)
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Supremissy
runners.spotlight: Has shown very little in her two completed starts and instantly opposable.
runners.comment: Has shown very little in her two completed starts and instantly opposable

runners.name: Baileys Khelstar
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner; showed the benefit of his return from a long absence when giving it a good go from the front in a 1m6f handicap at Wolverhampton 16 days ago; whether he can improve again is debatable and this is tougher.
runners.comment: Second run back from an absence when second on the AW; this looks tougher
runners.owner: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Exposed 6yo who is 7-27 on AW surfaces but 1-12 on turf; respectable runs of late at Musselburgh but wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: Respectable runs of late at Musselburgh but wouldn't be the obvious answer

runners.name: Galyx
runners.spotlight: 1m/1m2f winner; has yet to entirely convince with his stamina beyond his last winning trip but that concern is clearly not shared by his trainer and he returns from an absence with the yard going well; any rain would be welcomed.
runners.comment: Has stamina to prove but returns with his yard going well; any rain welcomed

runners.name: Square Necker
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; his debut third in a 1m2f Doncaster maiden last March (good to soft) worked out exceptionally well and he then won easily at Dundalk in December (1m4f; odds-on; had been gelded), at the expense of a Listed-placed rival; could be on a useful mark for handicap debut and when second to Constitution Hill at Southwell (1m4f novice) he was giving the winner 5lb.
runners.comment: When second to Constitution Hill at Southwell (1m4f novice) he was giving the winner 5lb

runners.name: Mountain Road
runners.spotlight: 4-15 for David Simcock; absent for 582 days after a heavy defeat on debut for this yard and last week's comeback run at Musselburgh was just okay (2m, good); others preferred.
runners.comment: Last week's comeback run at Musselburgh was just okay (2m, good); others preferred

runners.name: Elysian Flame
runners.spotlight: Smart staying handicapper on the Flat for Mick and David Easterby in 2021 when competitive at some of the major meetings; having only his second run since that year when winning well over 2m at Beverley last month (good to soft); still useful, clearly, and has only gone up 2lb.
runners.comment: Second run since 2021 when winning well over 2m at Beverley last month; up 2lb

races.race_name: Darley Stallions EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Conclave
runners.spotlight: Awarded his debut race after the first past the post weighed in light and that rival impressed next time out; this gelding also took a sizeable step forward back at Kempton (1m) when a clear second behind yesterday's 2,000 Guineas contender King's Trail; faces some interesting opponents here but might be up to the task.
runners.comment: Awarded debut race; better again when second to a class act back at Kempton

runners.name: Haayimm
runners.spotlight: Gelded after last season's run; highly unlikely that the 1-8 favourite gave his running when this son of Gleneagles beat him at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) but he's a well-bred improver and unexposed.
runners.comment: Beat a disappointing fav at Pontefract but this gelding is very much unexposed

runners.name: Ervani
runners.spotlight: Only made his debut last week when runner-up to a 1-4 chance at Wetherby (1m, good to firm); a line through the third gives him a fair bit to find with Conclave but this colt is clearly open to improvement.
runners.comment: Only made his debut last week when runner-up to a 1-4 chance at Wetherby; can improve

runners.name: Proud Nation
runners.spotlight: Sharp pedigree but was confined to 7f last season and made all second time out at York (good); mixed messages as to the strength of that form but he appeals as the type to do well as a 3yo.
runners.comment: Sharp pedigree but was confined to 7f last season and made all second time out at York

races.race_name: Aspire Cleaning & Facilities Throughout Scotland Handicap

runners.name: Starliner
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 3-42 and he's stuck on a lengthy losing run; reduced mark is warranted.
runners.comment: Strike-rate of 3-42 and he's stuck on a lengthy losing run; reduced mark is warranted
runners.owner: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt

runners.name: Arkenstaar
runners.spotlight: One of his seven wins here came in this race two seasons ago; had the benefit of a run that year and record after this sort of break isn't overly encouraging; however, his course record is too good to ignore, he's on a competitive mark and goes on all ground.
runners.comment: One of seven wins here came in this race two years ago; does lack a recent run this time

runners.name: Sanafi Zabeel
runners.spotlight: Progressive profile in a light German career, the win coming over 1m1f on good ground; bought for 20,000gns; not the easiest to weigh up and best to let the market guide.
runners.comment: Lightly raced German winner; not the easiest to weigh up and best to let the market guide

runners.name: Native Instinct
runners.spotlight: Novice winner on soft and has gone close in a 7f handicap on good to soft; well beaten upped to 1m at Leicester on final run in October but that was too bad to be true; has dipped to a dangerous mark and, at this trip, may have improvement lurking.
runners.comment: Has dipped to a dangerous mark and could yet prove his stamina over this far

runners.name: Izzari
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner who was quite eyecatching after a break at Newcastle (1m) and the winner has been going places; fine on the grass and considered.
runners.comment: Three-time winner who was quite eyecatching after a break at Newcastle (1m)

runners.name: Polygram
runners.spotlight: Went close more than once for Harry Charlton but left that yard an eight-race maiden and comfortably held in 7f/1m1f handicaps for current one; the return of cheekpieces might not be a bad thing (figures of 23 when tried previously) but there are safer options.
runners.comment: 0-8 for previous yard; comfortably held in 7f/1m1f handicaps for current one

runners.name: Native Honey
runners.spotlight: Had some high points last season on which he's realistically treated but his return last week at Musselburgh was underwhelming.
runners.comment: Realistically treated but his return last week at Musselburgh was underwhelming

runners.name: Quiet Resolve
runners.spotlight: AW winner; placed in the Legends race at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in September but never involved next time at Ayr, which made it 0-12 on grass; has since left Richard Fahey.
runners.comment: AW winner but 0-12 on grass and has left Richard Fahey since last seen in September

runners.name: Liverpool Star
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough last week at Ripon (6f, good) and the 4lb rise could have been worse as the front two pulled nearly 5l clear; obvious chance in this weak race.
runners.comment: Front two pulled clear last week at Ripon so the 4lb rise could have been worse

runners.name: Cosmic Clarets
runners.spotlight: Last season petered out after a promising start, but he was gelded prior to his comeback run at Newcastle (6f handicap) where he went down narrowly under a prominent ride; clear claims on the back of that after a 1lb nudge.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to comeback run and only went down by half a length at Newcastle

runners.name: Realistic Dream
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish filly; not beaten far on this week's stable debut at Musselburgh (5f, good) but still beat only one home.
runners.comment: Not beaten far this week at Musselburgh (5f, good) but still beat only one home

runners.name: Ellie's de Vega
runners.spotlight: In first-time blinkers when beaten 3.25l last week at Haydock (6f, good to firm; led) and that was in a Class 5 handicap after a break of 182 days; should run well.
runners.comment: Third at Haydock last week and that was in a Class 5 handicap after a break of 182 days

runners.name: Simba's Pride
runners.spotlight: Similar sort of RPRs in his qualifying runs, over 5f, 1m and 7f; this initial mark could be workable but doubts remain as to what his trip will be.
runners.comment: Similar sort of RPRs in his qualifying runs, over 5f, 1m and 7f; this mark could be okay

runners.name: Breeze Star
runners.spotlight: Offered little thus far, including two sprint nurseries; tongue-tie added for reappearance.
runners.comment: Offered little thus far, including two sprint nurseries; tongue-tie added for reappearance

runners.name: With Glory
runners.spotlight: Bought for 7,000gns in October after showing some ability at two, though the second came in a four-runner race; remote last on yard debut but that was a novice for which she was 80-1; may fare better on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Some ability at two; quiet comeback run but handicaps are now the way to go

runners.name: Dandy Orton
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat many rivals in five runs for Ollie Sangster, including two 5f nurseries when blinkered; new trainer fits a hood; too much to prove; 1lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Failed to beat many rivals in five runs for Ollie Sangster; new trainer fits a hood

races.race_name: Hampton By Hilton Hotel Hamilton Park Handicap

runners.name: Mount Ruapehu
runners.spotlight: Has been on a roll at Southwell in recent weeks and didn't run badly in defeat at Wolverhampton; carries a penalty for the cosy win five days ago but a greater concern would be his record on grass, which reads 1-33.
runners.comment: Has been on a roll on the AW in recent weeks but record on grass reads 1-33

runners.name: King Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Went winless last season but ran well on occasions and he's handicapped to go in at some point; seems to run well fresh.
runners.comment: Went winless last season but ran well on occasions and he's handicapped to go in

runners.name: Doon The Glen
runners.spotlight: The Newcastle win in March 2025 is his only victory in 29 starts; just one effort of note this year and that was in a classified race.
runners.comment: Only 1-29; just one effort of note this year and that was in a classified race

runners.name: Colour Code
runners.spotlight: Only 1-20; often misses the break and didn't even consent to race last time; too risky.
runners.comment: Only 1-20; often misses the break and didn't even consent to race last time; too risky

runners.name: Thunderstorm Katie
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins over 5f in October; lost her way in final three races but could be better for a break and has won here.
runners.comment: Lost her way in final three races but could be better for a break and has won here

runners.name: Digital
runners.spotlight: Won three 5f AW handicaps through the winter but his last three efforts were nothing to shout about; capable on turf but needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Three AW wins last winter but last three efforts were nothing to shout about
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Ganesha
runners.spotlight: Four wins divided between turf and the AW; some sign of returning to form from off the pace at Newcastle 20 days ago (5f); possible.
runners.comment: Some sign of returning to form from off the pace at Newcastle 20 days ago

runners.name: Opal Storm
runners.spotlight: Arrived late to win at Southwell (5f) and only faded late on at Doncaster last week (good to firm); remains in form and she is proven on a stiff 5f.
runners.comment: Arrived late to win at Southwell (5f) and only faded late on at Doncaster last week

runners.name: Sir Benedict
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner with bucketloads of placed efforts to his name; both runs for this yard have been respectable and the return of cheekpieces is a definite plus.
runners.comment: Both runs for this yard respectable and the return of cheekpieces is a definite plus

runners.name: Midnight Lir
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since 2023 and he beat one home when last seen out in October; one to watch after a break of 213 days.
runners.comment: Hasn't won since 2023 and he beat one home when last seen out in October

runners.name: Until Dawn
runners.spotlight: Both wins came on the AW in early 2025; her more recent efforts on either side of a wind operation have been thoroughly underwhelming.
runners.comment: Recent efforts on either side of a wind operation have been thoroughly underwhelming
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Sixcor
runners.spotlight: Ended the last turf campaign with some lesser efforts; others bring more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Ended the last turf campaign with some lesser efforts; others bring more pressing claims

runners.name: Hi Lord
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; won over 5f in 2024 but hard to recommend on exploits for current yard.
runners.comment: Won over 5f in 2024 but hard to recommend on exploits for current yard

runners.name: Alpine Sierra
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three consecutive times on Tapeta in March (1m-1m2f) and returned to turf with pretty good third at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) on Wednesday; should be fine over this longer trip; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Second three times on AW in March and also ran well when back on turf on Wednesday

runners.name: Jaminoz
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner, most recently over today's trip (good to soft) in September; ended 2025 with two below-par hurdle runs, though, and would appeal more on ground slower than good.
runners.comment: Four-time course winner; below form over hurdles in late 2025; best on slower ground

runners.name: Taylormade Lad
runners.spotlight: Came good with narrow win at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm) last summer, his seventh start; absent since disappointing AW run in first-time cheekpieces (retained) in October but has scope for progress this year.
runners.comment: Won over this trip at Carlisle last summer; has fitness to prove but not yet fully exposed

runners.name: Valley Of Flowers
runners.spotlight: Made the frame four consecutive times last July-September (1m3f-2m), but made a low-key reappearance last week and is probably better suited by a stiffer test of stamina than this now.
runners.comment: In good form last summer but made low-key return; may need a stiffer stamina test

runners.name: Newport
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on the Flat when trained in Ireland and not seen in this sphere since summer 2024, but has largely been in good form over hurdles for Iain Jardine since the autumn; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Not seen on Flat since 2024 but largely in good form over hurdles since the autumn

runners.name: Gozo
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on Flat form for Ben Brookhouse in early part of 2025, but pulled up in two handicap hurdles when owner-trained this year; now in the care of Mike Smith for Flat return; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Pulled up twice over hurdles this year; back on the Flat with lots to prove

runners.name: Monsieur Melee
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for 45,000gns after string of good efforts for Jedd O'Keeffe in summer 2024, but this stable debut has been a long time coming and he might come on for the run.
runners.comment: In good form for Jedd O'Keeffe in summer 2024; makes stable debut after long absence

runners.name: Ned Broy
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three maiden/novice hurdles since February, but made fairly encouraging Flat handicap debut when keeping-on sixth at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in January and remains unexposed; worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging handicap debut in January; safely held in three hurdles since

races.race_name: Find Us At fitzdares.com Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Moonhall Lass
runners.spotlight: 0-22 in Ireland, but has done well since joining James Owen, finishing a close third of seven at Southwell (6f, AW) last month (form worked out well) before getting off the mark at Yarmouth (6f, good) on Tuesday; 5lb penalty may not stop her, especially with Henry Callan's record this year reading 3112116114.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at Yarmouth on Tuesday; 5lb penalty may not stop her under in-form rider

runners.name: Unico
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than when winning at Brighton (5f, soft) last September (has won on good to firm); Simon Walker a positive, but may be better for the run after 134 days off.
runners.comment: Simon Walker a positive, but may be better for the run after 134 days off

runners.name: Rovinia
runners.spotlight: Mainly seen on the AW (1-8), but placed in all three starts on turf; has the assistance of one of the more experienced riders, but will need to improve on this month's Southwell (7f, AW) reappearance.
runners.comment: Placed in all three starts on turf; will need to improve on his Southwell reappearance

runners.name: Roman Spring
runners.spotlight: It took him a long time to get off the mark but he was successful three times last year; goes on any ground, but may just need this after five months off; stable also runs Beau Jardine.
runners.comment: Successful three times last year; may just need this after five months off

runners.name: Sub Thirteen
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 5f, but stays 6f well; 1lb below his last winning mark and has the assistance of an experienced amateur, but he could really do with rain.
runners.comment: 1lb below his last winning mark but could really do with rain

runners.name: Conquest Of Power
runners.spotlight: Rarely seen on turf these days even though he has won on it; only one piece of recent worthwhile form and jockey (who also owns him) is having her first ride in public; has dropped 8lb below his last winning mark, though, so could play a part if producing his best.
runners.comment: Has dropped 8lb below his last winning mark so could play a part if producing his best

runners.name: Alyara
runners.spotlight: Two of her three wins have come on turf, but seen much more often on the AW these days; latest efforts have been respectable, but a losing run of 26 sounds a note of caution.
runners.comment: Seen more often on the AW these days; latest efforts respectable but losing run up to 26
runners.owner: Lynn Cullimore Pete Ponting Joe Ponting

runners.name: Beau Jardine
runners.spotlight: Last two wins have come over C&D and he is 13lb lower than for the latest in May 2024 (0-24 since); has won fresh in the past and has run well on good to firm ground, but a softer surface suits him better.
runners.comment: Last two wins have come over C&D, but losing run up to 24; soft ground suits him best

races.race_name: Fitzdares Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Ziggy Starshine
runners.spotlight: Landed quite a gamble when making a successful debut at Bath (5f, good; 15-8) 16 days ago, beating a subsequent winner with a lot more in hand than the neck margin would suggest; major player.
runners.comment: Won with more in hand than margin would suggest on Bath debut: form franked; major player

runners.name: Passerine
runners.spotlight: Sent off 7-1 when beaten just over 4l into fourth of 13 behind Ziggy Starshine at Bath (5f, good) 16 days ago; 5lb better off and open to improvement, but it will be needed.
runners.comment: Fourth behind Ziggy Starshine on Bath debut; open to improvement but it will be needed

runners.name: Babzini
runners.spotlight: March foal by St Mark's Basilica; 100,000gns yearling; dam 7.6f winner (RPR 82), sister to UAE 1m1f Group 2 winner Valiant Prince, half-sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Klassique, out of 1m Group 1 winner; stable can get them ready first time, but she may appreciate further in due course.
runners.comment: Stable can get them ready first time, but she may appreciate further in due course

runners.name: Nonrem
runners.spotlight: February foal by Oasis Dream; £50,000 yearling; closely related to 6f 2yo winner Apache Green (RPR 79); dam unraced half-sister to Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint winner Belvoir Bay, out of 1m winner; bred for speed, but stable's 2yo newcomers are usually better for a run.
runners.comment: Bred for speed, but stable's 2yo newcomers are usually better for a run

runners.name: Rhodes Runner
runners.spotlight: March foal by Minzaal; 75,000gns yearling; dam maiden half-sister to winners Sumiha (1m4f Group 3) and Suwayra (1m 2yo), out of Group-placed 1m2f winning half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner Sinawann; bred to stay further but the stable has made a bright start to the season with its 2yos; respected.
runners.comment: Bred to stay further but the stable has made a bright start to the season with its 2yos

runners.name: Lover Girl
runners.spotlight: Overcame the outside stall when making a winning debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW; 11-2) this month; the form has been franked, but this looks harder.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut at Wolverhampton; form has been franked, but this looks harder

runners.name: Jax Edge
runners.spotlight: Dropped right away at Bath (5.7f, good) on her reappearance 16 days ago; can win more races this year but she is yet to defy a mark this high and there are in-form opponents.
runners.comment: Dropped right away on recent return to action (5.7f, good); needs to leave that behind

runners.name: Dapper Valley
runners.spotlight: Quickly coaxed back to form by James Owen last year, winning 6f handicaps at Kempton (twice) and Chester; struggled on dirt in the Middle East over the winter but his mark dropped quickly and he ran well back on turf in Abu Dhabi in March; returned to Britain with an emphatic success at Leicester (6f, good to firm) eight days ago; today's 8lb higher mark should still be in range but the drop to 5f is a slight query.
runners.comment: Returned to Britain off a reduced mark & won easily at Leicester 8 days ago; 5f a slight ?

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: Speedy filly who won three 5f handicaps after joining Grace Harris last year; raced too freely in the early stages of her return at Nottingham last month but it was a solid start to the year and she should make a bold bid despite stepping up in class.
runners.comment: Three 5f handicap wins last year; promising return last month; up in class today

runners.name: Em Four
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf last year (as well as once on AW) and he has continued on an upward curve in 2026, winning for the third time this season when making all at Wolverhampton 12 days ago; that was his first success at this trip and there could be more to come at 5f; 6lb rise demands progress though.
runners.comment: Career-best RPR dropped to 5f at Wolverhampton 12 days ago, making all; needs more up 6lb

runners.name: Ziggy's Missile
runners.spotlight: Most of his success has come on AW but he showed at Nottingham last spring that he could get the job done on turf (5f, good); looked as though his recent seasonal return would bring him on and his lacklustre end to 2025 has seen him drop to a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Most success on AW but has won on turf; sharper for recent reappearance; dangerous mark

races.race_name: Download The Fitzdares App Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Poetry Of Time
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in all four starts over 7f/1m on turf/AW last autumn; off six months having been gelded in the meantime; relatively exposed, but should still run his race.
runners.comment: Made the frame in all four starts last autumn; should run his race on return

runners.name: Storming Point
runners.spotlight: Sent off 15-2 when beaten a short head by a stablemate of Poetry Of Time on his Newbury debut (6.5f, soft) in October; the ground here is likely to be different, but that was still a promising start; respected.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head on Newbury debut in October; respected despite likely different ground

runners.name: Guitar Solo
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Finest Sound (Group placed 7f-1m2f including Saudi Arabia; RPR 114) and Megaphone (5f/6f AW; 83); dam unraced sister to Qatari 1m-1m2f winner Seema, out of unraced close relative of Grand Prix de Paris/St Leger winner Scorpion; enough in her pedigree to make her worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Enough in her pedigree to make her worth monitoring in the market
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: The Bureau Club
runners.spotlight: Fourth in both starts over 1m on the AW with the form of her latest Kempton performance having since received a few boosts; the drop in trip may not be completely ideal, but she still makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Fourth in both starts over 1m on AW; drop in trip may not be ideal, but still shortlisted

runners.name: Dream Vega
runners.spotlight: Sister to 7f-1m1f winner Dreamloper (including Group 1; RPR 121), half-sister to four winners including Santorini Star (1m-2m including AW/Group 2; 113), Dreamweaver (1m2f-1m6f including AW; 91) and Dreamasar (1m AW; 83); dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (including AW Listed; 99); beautifully bred and market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Beautifully bred and market support should be heeded
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill

runners.name: Seven Sisters
runners.spotlight: 475,000gns yearling; half-sister to five winners including Barney Roy (7f-1m4f including 2yo/Group 1; RPR 124), Noble Dynasty (7f/1m including 2yo/AW/Group 3; 118), Wisdom Mind (1m; 98) and Arabian Tribe (8.6f AW 2yo; 89); dam maiden (71), out of 7f/1m Group 3 winner; highly attractive pedigree and stable has won this race (or a division of it) twice since 2019, including with a newcomer.
runners.comment: Highly attractive pedigree and stable has won this race twice since 2019

runners.name: Binmalk
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam Listed-placed 7f 2yo/1m AW winner (RPR 106), sister to winners Benbatl (1m1f/1m2f Group 1) and Modaara (useful 1m2f/1m4f), half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Elmalka (by Kingman), out of 1m2f Group 1/Grade 1 winner; makes plenty of appeal on breeding and stable has a fine record with 3yo newcomers.
runners.comment: Makes plenty of appeal on breeding and stable has a fine record with 3yo newcomers

runners.name: Dream Camp
runners.spotlight: 250,000euros yearling; showed definite promise when fifth of 13 on his Kempton debut (7f, AW; 13-2) in December; bred to do better and worth considering, especially if the market is positive.
runners.comment: Definite promise when fifth on his Kempton debut in December; bred to do better

runners.name: Tass
runners.spotlight: Not much promise in his first two starts over 6f on the AW; looks more one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Not much promise in two starts over 6f on the AW; looks more one for handicaps

races.race_name: Call Fitzdares For Top Prices Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Siam Ruby
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of her four starts, namely front-running efforts in small fields at Lingfield (1m2f) and Wolverhampton (9.4f); her only turf outing saw her make a bold bid at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) in March; up 4lb for the latest win but she appeals as having more in the tank.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in small fields; ran well on sole turf outing; still has untapped potential

runners.name: Princess Rascal
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (good to firm) last May on her second start; ran well in all three handicaps over this trip (flopped over 1m4f) afterwards, winning at Newmarket in October off 6lb lower; she will need to improve again to defy this mark but after just six starts it is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Two 1m2f wins last year, including here; still has potential after just six starts; chance

runners.name: Royal Poetry
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt when making all in a steadily-run 8.6f fillies' novice at Wolverhampton last month; likely the runner-up underperformed on that occasion and the handicapper doesn't appear to have taken any chances; that said, she's an unexposed and well-bred filly who could benefit from today's step up in trip.
runners.comment: Made all in steadily-run 4-runner novice last month; unexposed but improvement essential
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Plaid
runners.spotlight: Plenty of speed on the bottom line of her pedigree but she is by Ghaiyyath and her races at around 1m have suggested this new, longer trip should be in range; up 2lb for a narrow defeat at Kempton five weeks ago but still a major player.
runners.comment: Promise in three runs at around 1m; any improvement for 1m2f will see her bang there

runners.name: Typical Woman
runners.spotlight: Ended her 2025 campaign with a 1m1f win at Lingfield (good to firm); never in the hunt on last month's return to action at Pontefract (1m, good to soft); vulnerable to improvers.
runners.comment: Low-key return at Pontefract last month; vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Dreamasar
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Group 1 winner Dreamloper; off the mark at the second attempt in 1m Lingfield maiden (AW) in September and backed it up with a good fourth under a penalty in 1m Yarmouth novice following month (race worked out well); has plenty more to offer now handicapping over this longer distance; has been a non-runner on account of good to firm and soft ground in the past.
runners.comment: Three promising runs at 1m as a 2yo; opening mark looks well in range and 1m2f should suit
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill

races.race_name: Fitzdares Extra Places Every Day EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Ranga Tang
runners.spotlight: The most exposed runner in the line-up, having run four times, but ran game race from the front when close third in Class 3 Newmarket handicap (1m2f, good; first-time cheekpieces) on last month's seasonal debut, and sets a useful standard back in a maiden; moves up in trip; headgear removed.
runners.comment: Close third in Class 3 Newmarket handicap last month (1m2f); sets useful standard

runners.name: Semper Femina
runners.spotlight: Made promising debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in March, finishing well to take second, and arguably ran quite a bit better than eventual 13l defeat suggests when ninth at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month; holds each-way claims at least here.
runners.comment: Finished well for second on debut and ran better than bare form suggests last time

runners.name: Scarlet Legend
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; brother to owner's Goodwood Cup winner Quickthorn, half-brother to five other winners (two useful); dam 7f-1m2f winner (RPR; 74); riding arrangements suggest Spirit Dreamer may be the stable's main hope.
runners.comment: Brother to owner's Goodwood Cup winner Quickthorn; makes debut today

runners.name: Spirit Dreamer
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros yearling; third foal; closely related to 1m4f AW winner Princess Rabab (RPR 67), half-brother to 1m4f winner Ancient Myth (81); dam 1m winner (77), sister to an Australian 1m Group 3 winner; market helpful.
runners.comment: Out of a sister to an Australian 1m Group 3 winner; market helpful before debut

runners.name: Last Trump
runners.spotlight: 80-1 for valuable Newbury maiden (1m3f, good) on debut a fortnight ago but shaped with significant promise before fading into fifth; has big role to play here if able to build on that performance.
runners.comment: Shaped with clear promise when fifth in valuable 1m3f Newbury maiden last month

runners.name: Outflank
runners.spotlight: 750,000gns yearling; brother to a Hong Kong Group 3 winner and closely related to 2,000 Guineas third Royal Scotsman; kept on for respectable third of five on debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW: winner looks smart) in December and has obvious scope for progress this year.
runners.comment: Has good pedigree and made promising debut on AW in December; can improve

runners.name: Sir Griflet
runners.spotlight: Has good pedigree but struggled on both outings last month (1m3f/1m2f) and can only be watched here.
runners.comment: Has good pedigree but struggled on both outings last month

runners.name: Shirakawa
runners.spotlight: Bettered low-key 2yo debut when seventh at Kempton (1m3f, AW) last month, where she ran well for a long way before weakening inside final furlong; finished behind Semper Femina there, though, and needs another big step forward here.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way at Kempton last month needs another big step forward here

runners.name: Surrey King
runners.spotlight: Out of a 1m4f Group 3 winner; 25-1, lost many lengths at the start and was always behind on debut at Newbury (1m, soft) in October.
runners.comment: 25-1, lost many lengths at the start and was always behind on debut in October (1m)

runners.name: Take The Boat
runners.spotlight: Came good for current stable at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) in February and has made the frame on three of her four AW starts since; could have untapped potential over staying trips but needs to prove herself on turf.
runners.comment: In good form on AW in recent months; now needs to prove herself on turf

runners.name: Sneaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: Looked as good as ever when keeping on well for narrow win at Wolverhampton (2m, AW) in March but his latest AW run was disappointing and his turf strike-rate is modest (1-15); others preferred.
runners.comment: As good as ever on AW two starts ago but has modest strike-rate on turf

runners.name: Golden Samba
runners.spotlight: Made low-key seasonal reappearance on Tapeta last month but was placed on handicap debut at Bath (1m6f, soft) in September and remains lightly raced; could have a say if suited by today's much faster ground.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; made low-key return in April but was placed on handicap debut in September

runners.name: Beggarman
runners.spotlight: Gained last season's two wins on consecutive starts at Ffos Las (1m6f/2m) in the summer; absent since heavy defeat in October and would appeal more on ground slower than good but resumes on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Absent since poor run in October but resumes on a workable mark
runners.owner: Mrs E Bickerton And A Streeter

runners.name: Basilette
runners.spotlight: Won two low-grade handicap hurdles in October; has fitness to prove after six-month absence and this is her first Flat start since September 2024 but she still enters calculations off a career-low mark.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner in October; back from a break today; first Flat run since 2024

runners.name: Kotari
runners.spotlight: Failed to win last year and is patently not as good as he was but he ran well over hurdles in March and reverts to the Flat on a tempting mark; might ideally prefer ground slower than good but still makes appeal.
runners.comment: Not the force of old but ran well over hurdles in March and returns to Flat on good mark

runners.name: Laravie
runners.spotlight: Developed into fairly useful hurdler when fitted with a visor last year, winning four times (2m4f-2m7f); didn't show a great deal in three AW novice races (8.6f-1m3f) in March and was soundly beaten when upped to 2m for recent handicap debut (visor refitted) but she may yet transfer some of that hurdling ability to this code.
runners.comment: Has made disappointing start to Flat career this year but is a fairly useful hurdler

races.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Matriarchal
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly sent off at 18-1 on debut in six runner maiden at Dundalk and was beaten just 2l into fourth despite racing keenly and running green; can improve for that and place claims if she acts on turf.
runners.comment: Beaten just 2l when green on debut at Dundalk, can improve, place shout if acts on turf

runners.name: Bit Of A Buzz
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when weakening late into a 5.75l third behind Force Noir at Naas six days ago; likely to improve for that first run and this easier 5f should suit; place shout.
runners.comment: Ability on debut over 5f at Naas, will improve, this easier 5f should suit, place shout

runners.name: Edward Thatch
runners.spotlight: Blackbeard colt sent off at even money on debut at the Curragh (5f, sft-hvy) but could only manage a 3.5l third of 8 after showing lots of early speed; pedigree suggests that better ground will suit, as will this easier 5f; leading chance.
runners.comment: Early speed on debut at the Curragh, better ground should suit here, leading chance
runners.owner: Giselle De Aguiar & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Sophia Royale
runners.spotlight: Soldier's Call filly; E7,000 yearling; sister to French 6.5f AW 2yo winner Finders Keepers; dam 5.7f turf/7f AW winner (RPR 84), half-sister to useful 6f-1m2f winner Mr Scaramanga; likely to improve for this initial experience.
runners.comment: Soldier's Call filly, sister to French 2yo winner, likely to improve for this experience

runners.name: Ballymackney
runners.spotlight: Earthlight filly; E20,000 yearling; dam unraced half-sister to Italian Listed winners Fast Spirit (7f/7.5f 2yo) and Bright Filly (6f 2yo), out of sister to winners Lethal Promise (5.5f/6f Listed) and Promising (Group-placed 6f); worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Earthlight filly, dam related to 2yo winners, worth monitoring in the market

runners.name: Queenofthewild
runners.spotlight: Bungle Inthejungle filly; E38,000 yearling; dam 5f winner (RPR 79), half-sister to winners One More Word (Listed-placed 7f-1m inc 2yo), Archers Road (5f inc 2yo) and Somalian (1m inc 2yo); worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Bungle Inthejungle filly, dam 5f winner, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Hanney Boy
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear colt; E30,000 yearling; brother to 6f/7f winner Noor Bano (inc UAE; RPR 97), half-brother to Qatari winners Tashkent (6f) and Ponga (7f 2yo); dam Listed-placed 7f-8.4f winner (inc 2yo; 102), half-sister to dam of 6f 2yo Group 1 winner Sioux Nation; nicely bred but yard not known for 2yo winners; watch.
runners.comment: Kodi Bear colt, dam listed placed 2yo winner, yard not known for juvenile winners

runners.name: Nation Blaze
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation colt; E52,000 yearling; dam unplaced 5f (RPR 44), half-sister to winners Parkes And Ride (Qatari 7f/1m), Cape Castle (1m4f-1m6f; dam of 1m2f Group 1 winner Thundering Nights) and Influent (1m/9.4f); respected source; check the market.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation colt, dam unplaced 5f, respected source, check the market

runners.name: Cyclone Code
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear colt; 34,000gns yearling; closely related to 1m AW 2yo winner Booziebrunch (RPR 73); dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo/6.5f winner (99), half-sister to winners Snap Shots (5f/6f inc 2yo) and Zebedaios (6f-1m); likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Kodi Bear colt, dam Listed placed 5f 2yo/6.5f winner, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Eighteenth Smiles
runners.spotlight: State Of Rest colt; dam 12.6f winner (RPR 83), closely related to 11.6f Listed winner Perfect Clarity, half-sister to Italian 1m2f Listed winner Law Power; pedigree suggests this trip may be too sharp.
runners.comment: State Of Rest colt, dam 12.6f winner, pedigree suggests this trip may be too sharp

runners.name: Santo Parvenue
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort on debut at the Curragh when a 12.5l fourth, green before making modest late gains; was 9l behind Edward Thatch, however, so much more needed to turn the tables on that rival.
runners.comment: Green and modest late gains on debut at the Curragh, was well behind Edward Thatch

runners.name: Santorini Storm
runners.spotlight: Soldier's Call colt beaten 20l on debut at the Curragh when well behind Edward Thatch; huge improvement needed to turn that form around.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l on debut when well behind Edward Thatch, huge improvement required

races.race_name: UNQUESTIONABLE Standing At Rathbarry Stud Maiden

runners.name: Sparky Sparky
runners.spotlight: Confirmed promise of Naas fourth last year with third placing over 5f here on reappearance; should not be far away, but collateral form involving Starman Tom suggests he might not cope with Cool Azul
runners.comment: Confirmed promise of Naas fourth last year with third placing over 5f here on reappearance

runners.name: Cool Azul
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners, notably Last Crusader, a 5f Listed scorer for Karl Burke; dam French Group-placed 6f 2yo winner; in front of useful yardstick Starman Tom (Listed-placed since) when second on debut in a 24-runner maiden at the Curragh; winner Thenandnow runs in the Listed race later on; could be hard to beat from a low draw.
runners.comment: In front of useful yardstick Starman Tom when second on debut, could be hard to beat now

runners.name: The Piper's Call
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 80-1 on debut for a Curragh maiden but belied those odds with an excellent second of 13 behind subsequent Group 3 runner-up Flushing Meadows; form advertised, notably by the top-class Puerto Rico; again showed winning potential when third behind a Ballydoyle-trained colt at Naas; gelded now.
runners.comment: Second on debut in a strong Curragh maiden, again showed winning potential at Naas

runners.name: Dreaminthejungle
runners.spotlight: Shaped promisingly when seventh of 16 over 6f at the Curragh on only start last year; fine effort against younger rivals when fifth of 24 at the same venue on reappearance; held by runner-up Cool Azul.
runners.comment: Fine effort against younger rivals when fifth of 24 at the Curragh, held by Cool Azul

runners.name: Kilmac Air
runners.spotlight: Did not show enough first time out at Gowran to suggest she can pose a serious threat.
runners.comment: Did not show enough first time out at Gowran to suggest she can pose a serious threat
runners.owner: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs M V Magnier

runners.name: Xerling
runners.spotlight: Fair 6f Naas maiden run on her second outing; out of her depth in a 5f Listed race at Dundalk last October; should be more at home in this company.
runners.comment: Fair 6f Naas maiden run, out of her depth in a 5f Listed race at Dundalk last October

runners.name: You Smiled
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister by Kessaar to an Italian 5f winner; dam 7f AW winner; stable's current form is a positive
runners.comment: Half-sister by Kessaar to an Italian 5f winner, dam 7f AW winner, stable in form

runners.name: Medusa King
runners.spotlight: 47,000gns yearling; eighth foal; brother to 5f Listed winner winner Dickiedooda, closely related to 1m4f AW winner Sioux Princess (69); dam French AW 2yo winner, half-sister to two Listed winners.
runners.comment: Brother to 5f Listed winner winner Dickiedooda, also closely related to a 1m4f AW winner

runners.name: Pacioli
runners.spotlight: 28,000gns yearling; Due Diligence gelding is the second foal out of 7f AW 2yo winner.
runners.comment: 28,000gns yearling. Due Diligence gelding is the second foal out of 7f AW 2yo winner.

runners.name: Samurai Mike
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling; third foal; gelded son of Starspangledbanner; dam Listed winner in Italy; could go well first time out.
runners.comment: Gelded son of Starspangledbanner, dam Listed winner in Italy, could go well first time out

runners.name: Trek Home
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-brother to Listed scorer Ocean Vision and two other winners; dam Listed-placed 7f winner, half-sister to US 7f Grade 2 winner/good broodmare Letgomyecho.
runners.comment: am Listed-placed 7f winner, half-sister to US Grade 2 winner/good broodmare Letgomyecho
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Casla's Eagle
runners.spotlight: Finished in mid-field over 1m at Gowran on debut, not guaranteed to improve over his trip.
runners.comment: Finished in mid-field over 1m at Gowran on debut, not guaranteed to improve over his trip

runners.name: Hasita
runners.spotlight: Needs to step up on the form of two juvenile outings at the Curragh to feature here.
runners.comment: Needs to step up on the form of two juvenile outings at the Curragh, yard's second string
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Cashel Blue
runners.spotlight: May improve from debut at this venue last month; has a bit to find with third-placed Sparky Sparky.
runners.comment: May improve from debut at this venue, has a bit to find with third-placed Sparky Sparky

runners.name: Concaire
runners.spotlight: No threat on AW on debut; down the field in Curragh race in which Cool Azul was second.
runners.comment: No threat on AW on debut, down the field in Curragh race in which Cool Azul was second

runners.name: Ragaire
runners.spotlight: 85,000gns foal, 400,000gns yearling; third foal; increase in purchase price from foal to yearling is noteworthy; however, made no show on debut in the Curragh race in which Cool Azul was second; 15-2 chance then, so that was presumably too bad to be true.
runners.comment: Fetched 400,000gns as a yearling, no show in Curragh race in which Cool Azul was second

runners.name: Ocean Echo
runners.spotlight: Slowly away/never in contention when 40-1 on debut in a 1m maiden at Gowran 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Slowly away/never in contention when 40-1 on debut in a 1m maiden at Gowran 11 days ago
runners.owner: R Scahill, S Begley, J & C Bourke

runners.name: Pick A Window
runners.spotlight: No sign of worthwhile form in three outings last season, two of them at this venue.
runners.comment: No sign of worthwhile form in three outings last season, two of them at this venue

races.race_name: Book Tickets Online At CorkRacecourse.ie Handicap

runners.name: Collecting Coin
runners.spotlight: Consistent form last season included a Galway maiden win (7f, yielding); sound effort on handicap debut and seasonal return at Naas (7f, soft); did not quite see out the 1m trip when a close-up fourth at Limerick eight days ago; return to 7f could make all the difference.
runners.comment: 7f winner, two solid runs this season, should be in the mix again with a similar display

runners.name: Aviatrice
runners.spotlight: Promoted to 95 on AW after Listed win at Dundalk last November, her third win from six starts at that track; runs off 7lb lower here; a likely player if ready, since she showed her effectiveness on turf with a couple of placed runs last August.
runners.comment: Promoted to 95 on AW after Listed win, runs off 7lb lower here, a likely player if ready

runners.name: Gleneagle Bay
