runners.spotlight: Sole win came on debut at Dundalk in March 2024; 0-12 since but ran well in top handicaps last season, including fifth in Irish Lincoln, third in Victoria Cup at Ascot and second in Ahonoora at Galway; flopped when fancied (5-1) on return in race won by Key Witness; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Has won only once but boasts solid form in big handicaps, flopped when fancied on return

runners.name: Dmaniac
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at the Curragh on final start of 2025 and reappeared with another 6f win at the same venue; beaten favourite over 5f last time; winning form points to a preference for testing conditions.
runners.comment: Winning form at the Curragh points to a preference for testing ground, bestsuited by 6f

runners.name: Key Witness
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden winner on the AW for Archie Watson; has prospered at the Curragh since joining Pat Flynn, ending last season with a 7f win and starting the new campaign with a 1m success; goes well for this rider whose claim is now reduced to 5lb.
runners.comment: Ended last term with a Curragh win, scored again there on return, may continue to improve

runners.name: Brewing
runners.spotlight: All six wins have been gained on AW; in good form at Dundalk this winter before a below-par Newcastle run on latest; others have much stronger claims on turf form.
runners.comment: All six wins have been gained on AW, others have much stronger claims on turf form

runners.name: Dos Mukasan
runners.spotlight: Four wins in France; testing ground conditions not ideal on his Irish debut at the Curragh; tough task under a big weight here.
runners.comment: Four wins in France, testing ground conditions not ideal on his Irish debut at the Curragh

runners.name: Genuine Article
runners.spotlight: Only two wins from 22 Flat starts, placed nine times; below best on seasonal debut in Curragh event won by today's rival Key Witness.
runners.comment: Only two wins from 22 Flat starts, placed nine times, below best on seasonal debut

runners.name: Va Va Vroom
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner at two; struggled in handicaps in the first half of last season for Jessica Harrington and never counted on yard debut last October; not much better on reappearance.
runners.comment: Ran in good company after a 7f maiden win at two, handicap form is unconvincing

runners.name: Quatre Bras
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner (twice over the extended 1m2f at Dundalk); out of form on turf last season but some good runs this term, second over this trip at Naas and dead-heating for fourth over 1m2f at Leopardstown despite not getting the clearest of runs; down the field behind Final Voyage here nine day ago.
runners.comment: Three wins on AW, yet to score on turf, down the field here since a good Leopardstown run

runners.name: Nancy J
runners.spotlight: No further success since debut Gowran win at two (7f, good to firm); highly tried on occasions; ran creditably in the Sandringham at Royal Ascot last June; formerly with Jessica Harrington; in rear at the Curragh a month ago on first run for Johnny Murtagh.
runners.comment: No further success since debut 7f Gowran win at two, in rear at the Curragh a month ago

runners.name: Dark Viper
runners.spotlight: Last run was on the ice at St Moritz; only two wins have been at Leopardstown over 1m; Collecting Coin is more likely to feature for the stable.
runners.comment: Last run was on the ice at St Moritz, Collecting Coin is more likely to feature for stable

runners.name: Gerrit's Gem
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come at this venue, twice over 6f, the other coming at 1m; liking for the track is enough to make him worth considering on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: All three wins have come at this venue, worth considering on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Final Voyage
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing sequence with a course win for this rider over the extended 1m2f nine days ago; 5lb higher and sharply down in trip; possibly vulnerable.
runners.comment: Ended a long losing sequence with course win for this rider, 5lb higher and down in trip

races.race_name: Goffs Irish EBF Polonia Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Havana Anna
runners.spotlight: Maiden and Listed winner over 5f; beaten only a nose in a Group 3 over the minimum trip at Longchamp, though produced her best effort when chasing home True Love in the 6f Group 1 Cheveley Park; failed to cope with the challenge of the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; the clear pick on overall form.
runners.comment: Listed winner/Group 3 second, second in Cheveley Park, can be forgiven Breeders' Cup run

runners.name: Ipanema Queen
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Curragh maiden on debut but seemed better suited to 5f as the season progressed; won two Listed races at the Curragh and Dundalk over the minimum trip after finishing 3 3/4l second to Havana Anna at Naas; lost out narrowly to a longer-priced stablemate at Dundalk on seasonal debut; should reach the money.
runners.comment: No match for Havana Anna at Naas, has solid Listed-winning form, went close on AW in March
runners.owner: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Thenandnow
runners.spotlight: Officially rated 75 after four runs at two, the best of which was a 1 3/4l second of eight on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good-to-firm); now promoted to 85 after winning a 24-runner maiden at the Curragh; that was not a strong race, and she faces a tough task now.
runners.comment: Won a 24-runner maiden at the Curragh, needs a lot more now judged on her official rating

runners.name: Royal Bay Cen
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner in France at two; posted her two best efforts in defeat in a 7f Group 3 at Longchamp and a 6f Group 3 at Chantilly; has never run over 5f, but her dam Royal Razalma won the Cornwallis.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner in France at two; posted her two best efforts in defeat over further

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Has run 11 times, with two of her three wins gained over C&D; failed to progress from a maiden win last May; sold for 9,000GBP in January and got back to form in winning a 5f Naas handicap on stable debut; fifth in a 6f handicap at the Curragh early here last month; reappeared over C&D the following day to win a 5f conditions race; more on her plate.
runners.comment: Lost form after C&D win last May, has got her career back on track with two wins this term

runners.name: Quiet Mutiny
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 6f winner from the family of outstanding sprinter Lochsong; justified good support on debut in a 6f maiden at Fairyhouse last July; the form could hardly have worked out better, runner-up Precise won her next four races, completing a Group 1 double in the Moyglare and Fillies' Mile.
runners.comment: Beat subsequent dual Group 1 winner Precise over 6f on only start at two, smart prospect

runners.name: Rosie Frith
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden winner last July on the fourth of eight stats at two; beaten a nose in an auction series race over 6f at this venue before going down by a neck in a 5f Dundalk event; signed off with a nursery win at Dundalk; struggled on her only run in a stakes race.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, solid form later but struggled on her only run in a stakes race

runners.name: Tornado Kiss
runners.spotlight: Narrowly beaten twice in Naas fillies' maidens before landing the odds in a seven-runner C&D maiden last September; outpaced in the closing stages in a 5f Listed race at Dundalk in October; not suited by the race conditions, but stable in good form.
runners.comment: Outpaced in the closing stages in a 5f Listed race at Dundalk last October, tough task

runners.name: Dawn Romance
runners.spotlight: Beaten 6l by Havana Anna over 5f at Naas on her debut; won a 6f maiden at the same venue on good ground on her third start; in rear in a 6f Listed race at Fairyhouse in September.
runners.comment: No match for Havana Anna on debut, 6f Naas winner, in rear in a Listed race at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Bear On The Run
runners.spotlight: Started with a respectable fifth of 14 at the Curragh last May; made all to win over the same trip at Dundalk on August on her only other start at two; half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Dun Na Sead, worth a shot at this.
runners.comment: Dundalk 6f winner, half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Dun Na Sead, worth a shot at this
runners.owner: Kildare Racing Syndicate & Kilmichael Racing Syndicate

races.race_name: HORSE AND JOCKEY HOTEL Cork Derby (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Happy Pharoah
runners.spotlight: A Leopardstown specialist, winning three times there last season over 1m5f, including a premier handicap in September; never a factor in the Irish Cesarewitch; needs to step up from his seasonal debut at the Curragh.
runners.comment: All his best performances have been at Leopardstown, stays further than this

runners.name: Star Harbour
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, on a long losing sequence now; unplaced in two outings this term; others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner, on a long losing sequence now, others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Genoah
runners.spotlight: Four wins on AW, has raced only four times on turf; plenty to prove in this surface but has to be taken seriously off this mark, 10lb below AW rating.
runners.comment: Four wins on AW, has raced only four times on turf, plenty to prove in this surface

runners.name: Navy Waters
runners.spotlight: Won a Down Royal maiden last May; added a 1m5f handicap at the same venue (beating today's rival Alba Chiara) and then first past the post but demoted at Leopardstown; began the season with a fair third over 1m6f at Navan; this shorter trip and better ground may help her add to her tally.
runners.comment: Began the season with a fair third at Navan, this shorter trip and better ground may help

runners.name: Thatwilldoso
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well last summer with wins at Roscommon and Galway; held her form well to record seconds at Sligo and Naas; should be better for a recent run at Leopardstown; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a fine second at Naas, should be better for a run at Leopardstown

runners.name: Taipan
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner has yet to score in a handicap; won a 1m6f conditions race at Galway for the second year running last August; poor run over 1m6f on seasonal debut; stable appears to have two stronger contenders.
runners.comment: Five-time winner has yet to score in a handicap, poor run over 1m6f on seasonal debut

runners.name: Perry Mason
runners.spotlight: His good Curragh form late last season and early this term has been on very soft ground; also testing when he won at Galway last October; any rain would improve his prospects, further drying would be a negative.
runners.comment: His good Curragh form has been on very soft ground, rain would improve his prospects

runners.name: Kilmeaden
runners.spotlight: Recent hurdle runs have been disappointing; ended last Flat season with a good 1m6f effort, taking second behind the subsequent Fred Winter winner Saratoga at Listowel.
runners.comment: Recent hurdle runs have been disappointing, ended last Flat season with a good 1m6f effort

runners.name: Mocking
runners.spotlight: Fair placed form early last season and won at Roscommon in the autumn; did not get the best of runs over 1m2f on seasonal debut,; probably more effective at this trip.
runners.comment: Did not get the best of runs over 1m2f on seasonal debut, more effective at this trip

runners.name: Immutable
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle and three-time Flat winner; found 1m2f inadequate on seasonal debut; extra couple of furlongs here should help.
runners.comment: Found 1m2f inadequate on seasonal debut, extra couple of furlongs here should help

runners.name: Londonofficecallin
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m6f conditions race and two races over hurdles in 2025; lacks a recent outing and possibly more effective as a hurdler at this stage of his career.
runners.comment: Won a 1m6f conditions race and two races over hurdles in 2025, lacks a recent outing

runners.name: Rock Of Ireland
runners.spotlight: On the upgrade on first two 2026 turf runs; 5lb out of the handicap; more needed.
runners.comment: Reserve, on the upgrade on first two 2026 turf runs, 5lb out of the handicap, more needed

runners.name: Lifting Sails
runners.spotlight: Struggled with the surface at Lingfield last November; showed plenty of ability on turf with a wide-margin maiden win at Bellewstown, and is relatively unexposed; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Struggled with the surface at Lingfield last November, has shown plenty of ability on turf
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Alba Chiara
runners.spotlight: All her best form has been at Down Royal, a win and two seconds, one of them behind today's rival Navy Waters; sent on a long journey from Northern Ireland for this; could go well.
runners.comment: All her best form has been at Down Royal, sent on a long journey for this, could go well
runners.owner: D W Sloan & C G Bryson & Mrs Tracey O'Hare

runners.name: Sea Coral
runners.spotlight: Possible excuse for a poor Dundalk run last October, maiden win was over 1m4f on good; stable possibly has a better chance with Lifting Sails.
runners.comment: Possible excuse for a poor Dundalk run last October, maiden win was over 1m4f on good

runners.name: Comfort Zone
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner went close in 2m4f Ascot Stakes last June; finished well when close third in 2m Naas handicap in October; this trip is on the short side.
runners.comment: Most of his best form reveals a preference for staying trips, third in 2025 Ascot Stakes

runners.name: Zoffman
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose 7yo whose losing run on the Flat stretches back to October 2022; hard to be confident against that background; yard in form.
runners.comment: Last Flat success was in 2022, hard to be confident against that background, yard in form

runners.name: Helvic Dream
runners.spotlight: Last year's Galway Hurdle runner-up, a long time since the last of his five Flat wins (enjoyed highlight in the Group 1 Tattersalls Gold Cup in 2021); slipping in the ratings and fit from hurdling.
runners.comment: Last year's Galway Hurdle runner-up, a long time since the last of his five Flat wins
runners.owner: Mrs Caroline Hendron & Mrs M Cahill

runners.name: Truth Be Told
runners.spotlight: Appreciated to step up in trip when winning at Thurles on his final start last season; further improvement possible for his new trainer.
runners.comment: Appreciated to step up in trip when winning on his final start last season, new yard now

races.race_name: Racing Again Sunday, May 10th Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Grey Intentions
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante's best maiden run her latest at Leopardstown (1m, gd-yld) but was still beaten 11l; likely best watched here unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, best maiden run on latest, watch unless the market speaks
runners.owner: Thomas J Quinn & Brian P Whelan

runners.name: Jojo's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens last season, including when fourth of 16 over C&D (yielding); two maiden runs this term below that level and now switches to a handicap for the first time; yard in form and potentially well treated on the form of that C&D run last year; check the market.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens including over C&D, potentially well treated, check the market

runners.name: Johnny Soda
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens last year at 7f-1m; left disappointing seasonal return behind to finish 1.75l behind Arctic Assassin at the Curragh (1m, soft) two weeks ago; should get closer on these terms; big chance.
runners.comment: 1.75l behind Arctic Assassin at the Curragh, should get much closer on these terms

runners.name: Coincidental Glory
runners.spotlight: Kept busy at two, winning one of his 10 starts; that win came at Dundalk (7f) in December off 72; went close in a 1m turf claimer on seasonal return at Gowran (soft); dropped 4lb for poor run at Naas (6f, good) six days ago; Johnny Soda looks yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Busy at two, won at Dundalk in Dec', below best latest, Johnny Soda yard's best chance
runners.owner: Total Recall Racing Club & J Fairhurst & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Harmani
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of three maidens; beaten 5.5l on debut when runner up at Navan (6f, soft) behind Charles Darwin; 3-1 favourite but faded when beaten 7.75l on seasonal return in a Gowran maiden (7f, soft); sharper for that and should get 1m on breeding; could go well on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant, promise in maidens, sharper for latest, should stay 1m

runners.name: L L Koulsty
runners.spotlight: Course winner last season at 6f (yld-sft); respectable run in 1m Newcastle novice (AW) in February and pleasing return to turf on handicap debut at the Curragh two weeks ago (7f, soft); step back up to 1m a plus; cheekpieces retained; big player off 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Course winner (6f), pleasing h'cap debut 14 days ago, step up to 1m a plus, player

runners.name: Kalir
runners.spotlight: Promise in all three maidens at 1m-1mf on  good/soft/AW; only beaten 1.5l when third of 10 at Dundalk on seasonal return last month and now switches to handicap company; could be well treated on the form of his two turf runs last season; can't discount.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant, potentially well treated on two turf runs last season, can't discount
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Venetian Star
runners.spotlight: Best effort last season a 1.5l second on handicap debut at Down Royal (7f, good); 4l behind Arctic Assassin on seasonal return at the Curragh; weighted to close the gap to that rival and should strip fitter but might prefer quicker ground.
runners.comment: 4l behind Arctic Assassin latest, mark unchanged, strip fitter, might need faster ground

runners.name: Bear Right
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens and similar story on handicap debut at Naas last September (1m, soft) when an 11l sixth of 16; met trouble in running when clipping heels and unseating on return at Limerick eight days ago; yard in form and too early to write off.
runners.comment: Modest form in maidens and on h'cap debut, clipped heels latest, too soon to write off

runners.name: Arctic Assassin
runners.spotlight: Left ordinary maiden form behind to win a 1m Curragh handicap (soft) by 0.75l on seasonal return two weeks ago, beating a couple of these rivals in the process; 9lb rise partly negated by useful rider's 4lb claim; should go well again.
runners.comment: H'cap debut winner on seasonal return, rider's claim partly negates 9lb rise, player

runners.name: Matilda Joslyngage
runners.spotlight: Modest form first two maidens at 7f but big step forward upped to 1m at Thurles (gd-yld) last October when a 1.75l runner up behind Killashee Warrior (now rated 94); could go well on handicap debut if ready after a 206-day absence.
runners.comment: Best maiden run when upped to 1m at Thurles, go well on h'cap bow if ready after break

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Smullen Series)

runners.name: Yousaynothingatall
runners.spotlight: Rated 80 after four starts; okay form in maidens at 1m-10.5f; narrowly denied on handicap debut upped to 1m4f here last month; drop back to this trip a plus on the evidence of that effort; big player.
runners.comment: Rated 80, narrowly denied over 1m4f here on h'cap bow latest, drop in trip a plus

runners.name: Lake Bonney
runners.spotlight: Encouraging effort on debut at Leopardstown (1m, yld-sft) last month, keeping on from midfield for a 7.75l fourth of 14 (2.75l ahead of Miss Romagna who was hampered); should improve for that and she's related to plenty of winners; dam won at 1m2f; in the mix.
runners.comment: Encouraging effort on debut at Leopardstown, improvement likely upped in trip, player
runners.owner: Barry Fowler & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Dreamkeeper
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 80-1 on debut at Navan (1m2f, gd-yld) but ran a promising race to finish sixth of 18, keeping on well; likely to improve plenty for that initial experience and yard remains in fine form; chance.
runners.comment: Belied odds of 80-1 on debut with fine run, should improve, yard in form, chance
runners.owner: Mrs H Shanahan & Mrs Jane Shanahan

runners.name: Spa Rose
runners.spotlight: Elzaam filly; dam unraced half-sister to winners The Navigator (1m-1m6f Flat and prolific 2m-2m5f hurdle), Mustique (6f 2yo) and Renaissance (5f-1m inc 2yo); tongue tied and likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Elzaam filly, dam unraced, tongue tied, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Churchfield Lady
runners.spotlight: Churchill filly; 20,000gns yearling; closely related to Italian 6f winner Soaring Eagle; dam French 7f winner (RPR 74), sister to US 6f/6.5f winner Consortium, out of US 8.5f/1m1f Grade 3 winner; this trip might stretch stamina on breeding.
runners.comment: Churchill filly, closely related to Italian 6f winner, trip might stretch stamina

runners.name: Mare Crisium
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon filly; E55,000 yearling; half-sister to yard's Group-placed 1m winner Queen Of Thunder (RPR 109); dam 1m2f AW winner (91), half-sister to smart 6.5f/7f winner Parfait; nicely bred and market can guide.
runners.comment: Sea The Moon filly, half-sister to yard's black type filly Queen Of Thunder, check market

runners.name: Palina
runners.spotlight: Japan filly; E20,000 yearling; half-sister to Czech/Slovak 1m2f/1m4f winner Paras; dam twice-raced German 1m winner, half-sister to German 1m Listed winner Peace Of Rose; market best guide.
runners.comment: E20,000 Japan filly, dam German 1m winner, market best guide on debut

runners.name: Aravalli
runners.spotlight: Beaten cumulative distance of 44l in maidens at Leopardstown (1m, yielding) last year and the Curragh (1m2f, heavy) on seasonal return; much more needed.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l at Leopardstown and 24l at the Curragh, much more needed

runners.name: Contrite
runners.spotlight: Well-related Teofilo filly outran her odds of 16-1 when a 5.25l fourth of 14 on debut at Dundalk in November (1m); bred to stay this far and worth a market check on seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: Decent fourth on debut over 1m at Dundalk, bred to stay this far, check market

runners.name: Lady Kelly
runners.spotlight: Modest form so far in maidens at the Curragh (7f, sft-hvy) last October and at Leopardstown (1m, yld-sft) last month; moves up in trip; others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Modest form in both maiden starts, upped in trip, others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Miss Romagna
runners.spotlight: Traffic problems when beaten 10.5l at Leopardstown on debut (2.25l behind Lake Bonney); not given a hard race after meeting interference 2f out; would be no surprise if she reversed form with Lake Bonney granted a clearer run here.
runners.comment: Met interference when behind Lake Bonney on debut, could turn that form around

runners.name: Zitkala Sa
runners.spotlight: Moderate efforts in maidens at Dundalk and Limerick last month; will find her level in handicaps.
runners.comment: Moderate efforts in maidens at Dundalk and Limerick, looks one for handicaps

runners.name: Themis
runners.spotlight: 12-1 for debut at the Curragh (7f, good) last August and failed to beat a rival home; bred to do better than that at some stage but McMonagle jumps ship.
runners.comment: Last of 10 on debut at the Curragh last season, McMonagle jumps ship

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Frank Pickle
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on the Flat and rated 74; went closest when beaten a head at the Curragh off this mark last August (1m4f, good); beaten 7.5l when fourth in 1m handicap at the Curragh on latest; place chance but wouldn't want to see much rain.
runners.comment: 0-7, ran well in 1m4f (good) handicap at the Curragh last year, place chance if no rain

runners.name: Atomic City
runners.spotlight: Identical RPRs of 75 for sole start last season (1m, heavy) and seasonal return when beaten 0.75l into third of 8 over 1m1f on bottomless ground, both at the Curragh; McMonagle takes over now and should improve for better ground and the step up in trip; leading chance.
runners.comment: RPRs of 75 for both starts, narrowly denied latest, improve for better ground, big player

runners.name: Maxminelli
runners.spotlight: Rated 69 after three maiden runs at 7f-1m2f; beaten 11.5l when seventh of 13 at Navan on latest (1m2f, heavy); new tongue tie needs to eke out much more.
runners.comment: Rated 69 after three maiden runs at 7f-1m2f, tongue tie needs to eke out much more

runners.name: Gaelic Drills
runners.spotlight: Offered something to build on with a keeping on 6.75l fifth of 11 on debut at Leopardstown (1m2f, yld-sft) 18 days ago; open to plenty of improvement and should go well.
runners.comment: Pleasing debut effort at Leopardstown, can improve for that, should go well

runners.name: Hard Promises
runners.spotlight: Pinatubo colt; E32,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Sidiriya (1m4f; RPR 86) and Sidiza (1m2f; 83); dam 9.4f winner (91), out of 11.6f Listed winner, from family of Derby and Arc winner Sinndar; plenty to like on paper and stable can ready one; check market.
runners.comment: Pinatubo colt, dam 9.4f winner from family of Sinndar, yard can ready one, check market

runners.name: Roi Du Nord
runners.spotlight: French recruit; beaten 0.75l over 1m4f on the AW on sole start for Francis Graffard in March 2025; bought for E6,000 at Arqana in July; best watched on seasonal return and stable debut after absence unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Promise on sole AW start in France, bought for E6,000, best watched unless market speaks

runners.name: Roaming Coyote
runners.spotlight: Long odds and soundly beaten in maidens at the Curragh last October and here last month; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: No impact in maidens here and at the Curragh, qualifies for handicaps after this

runners.name: Amrum
runners.spotlight: Well-bred but failed to get involved when beaten 9.5l on debut in a 1m Dundalk maiden last month; needs to improve plenty upped in trip but it's still very early days so that's not impossible.
runners.comment: Well-bred but no impact over 1m on Dundalk debut, must improve, still early days

runners.name: Silver Cuff
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeats at Leopardstown and over this C&D on latest; others preferred.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats at Leopardstown and over this C&D, others look stronger

runners.name: Musical Master
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and ran accordingly in two maidens at Gowran and over C&D last month; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Huge odds and no show in two maidens last month, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Sunrush
runners.spotlight: Yard in form but this colt produced two moderate efforts in maidens last season at Tipperary (7f, yielding) and the Curragh (7f, yld-sft); probably best watched upped in trip on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Moderate form in two runs last season, moves up in trip, probably best watched

runners.name: Dreamed To Dream
runners.spotlight: Rating of 67 flatters him on what he has shown in four Flat starts; heavy defeat over hurdles latest; now with a fourth different trainer and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Rating of 67 flatters him, with fourth different trainer now, hard to fancy

runners.name: Abbotsford
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt was sent off at just 9-2 on debut at Dundalk in December (1m) but missed the break and never really threatened; surely capable of better and bred to stay this longer trip but a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Disappointing debut at Dundalk in Dec', surely capable of better but a bit to prove

races.race_name: Download The Tote App Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Aughlish
runners.spotlight: Useful level of form on latest two of three AW starts, showing plenty of pace when close third over 6f and looked in need of a return to this trip when staying-on second to a clear winner over 5f 10 days ago (from a wide draw); cheekpieces now fitted, and likely more required here on turf debut.
runners.comment: Ability in AW maidens of late; more likely required on turf debut with cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Glen And Tonic
runners.spotlight: Busy 2yo campaign, placed a couple of times on Dundalk AW; at least as good form on last month's seasonal return at the Curragh when beaten under 5l on soft; more needed to play a major role here.
runners.comment: Curragh comeback run promising but more needed to play a major role here

runners.name: Baiana
runners.spotlight: First foal; 100,000euros yearling; dam unraced half-sister to useful German 6f-7f winner Blueridge Silver, out of South African 1m Grade 1 winner; much improved on second outing last October, speed throughout when beaten 0.5l in Navan auction maiden over this trip (Jackie Jump Up a tenderly-ridden neck away third); can go well if handling better ground.
runners.comment: Navan second augurs well; contender if handling better ground

runners.name: Jackie Jump Up
runners.spotlight: Much the better of three late-season 2yo outings on final occasion when strong-finishing third (neck off runner-up Baiana) in Navan auction maiden in first-time hood, prompting running and riding enquiry; yard have begun the season in fine form, should go well.
runners.comment: Tenderly-ridden neck off Baiana at Navan; yard have begun the season in fine form

runners.name: Andaman Fifty
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier filly cost 90,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner Defiance (RPR 105); dam placed 7f/1m (76), half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Magic Artist, out of half-sister to St Leger and Italian Derby winner Mastery; top yard, newcomer demands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Palace Pier filly cost 90,000euros as a yearling; top yard, demands plenty of respect

runners.name: Star Reign
runners.spotlight: Some promise in 5f turf maidens for Gordon Elliot at the outset of her career; well held in both handicaps this term, and hard to see her having an impact back in maiden company.
runners.comment: Well held in both handicaps this term and hard to see her having an impact back in maiden

runners.name: Land Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Plum last on both 2yo starts and easily passed over on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Plum last on both 2yo starts and easily passed over on seasonal return

runners.name: Enchanted Lady
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly was a E38,000 yearling; half-sister to 1m4f winner Cycladic (RPR 99); dam 1m2f/1m4f winner (101); always behind after slow start on recent Limerick debut; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Always behind after slow start on recent Limerick debut; best watched for now

runners.name: Fate's Gambit
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Dundalk (7f) in January but hasn't reproduced that level of form in three runs since, not getting home on soft/heavy the last twice at the Curragh and Cork; return to better ground should suit and this doesn't look a strong maiden.
runners.comment: Return to better ground should suit and this doesn't look a strong maiden
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell

runners.name: County Carlow
runners.spotlight: 170,000gns yearling; favourite, plenty green when runner-up on debut in Dundalk maiden in late March (7f), following month at same venue forced pace (unbalanced 2f out) but didn't get home over a mile; cheekpieces now fitted and marked drop in trip.
runners.comment: Didn't get home over 1m latest; cheekpieces now fitted and marked drop in trip

runners.name: In The Gloaming
runners.spotlight: 33,000euros yearling; half-brother to two winners (5f to 6f, inc Group-placed); last of 12 on debut at the Curragh in September and forced much of pace on seasonal return before fading on soft ground at Gowran last month; probably more one for handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Didn't get home on soft at Gowran; probably more one for handicaps in due course
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.name: Keogie
runners.spotlight: 21,000euros yearling; half-brother to Czech 7f 2yo/1m3f winner Riwetta; promising Curragh debut in March when staying-on second (4.5l ahead of third-placed Fate's Gambit) over this trip (heavy); didn't get home over a mile under forcing tactics at Gowran last month; return to this trip to suit, and could go well if handling better ground.
runners.comment: Didn't stay 1m latest, previous Curragh second gives him a definite chance

runners.name: Gia Mor
runners.spotlight: Two juvenile runs came in good-quality maidens in first half of season on good ground, not discredited at both the Curragh and Cork; this looks a much weaker maiden so could be interesting if forward enough.
runners.comment: Some promise much better-class 2yo maidens early last season; don't rule out on return

runners.name: Little Roy
runners.spotlight: Galileo Gold gelding; unraced dam closely related to US 1m Grade 2 winner Ryehill Dreamer; yard not known for Flat maiden winners; watch.
runners.comment: Newcomer likely best watched.

runners.name: Classical Symphony
runners.spotlight: Very lightly-raced 4yo has shown nothing in three starts.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 4yo has shown nothing in three starts

runners.name: Basilano
runners.spotlight: Well-beaten last in maidens at the Curragh and Dundalk.
runners.comment: Well beaten last in maidens at the Curragh and Dundalk

runners.name: Substance
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to useful 6f/7f winners Ejtilaab and Eqtiraan (Listed); okay Curragh debut last summer behind a stablemate but didn't improve as expected at Listowel, finding stamina test (1m, heavy) beyond him; back in trip for seasonal return and don't rule out.
runners.comment: Back in trip for seasonal return and don't rule out

races.race_name: Tote Never Beaten By SP Handicap

runners.name: My Kurkum
runners.spotlight: Seasonal debut second in this race last year came from out of the blue, but he followed it up with another second at Roscommon before losing his form again; no show in two runs this year (excuse last time); different headgear tried.
runners.comment: Last year's second in this came out of the blue, also second next time; lost form since

runners.name: Happy Henry
runners.spotlight: Strong-travelling maiden went close in 19-runner handicap over 6f at Navan in the autumn, and returned with good effort there in March when just behind Ukiyo (and in front of Polar Bear and Gegenpressing); again found little when let down at Bellewstown latest and head carriage not endearing; might prefer it softer too.
runners.comment: Strong-travelling maiden has run well both starts this season, but can finish tamely

runners.name: Gegenpressing
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in three runs at Sligo and going for three-in-a-row in this race, the last two coming from stall 16; Hayes is elsewhere today, but quite the substitute to have in the saddle and his comeback run was fine.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in three runs at Sligo and going for three-in-a-row in this race; nice comeback

runners.name: Carrickfinn
runners.spotlight: Donegal-trained prominent racer is modest maiden, but nearly landed a gamble over 1m at Gowran last summer and good effort on AW after; money for him on return when missed break, and jockey booking looks significant.
runners.comment: Modest maiden, but nearly landed a gamble over 1m at Gowran last year and good jockey up

runners.name: Rattletheonionbag
runners.spotlight: Lots of placed form on AW at Dundalk, including when beaten only half-a-length last month, but still a maiden and her turf form isn't as good.
runners.comment: Lots of placed form on AW, including close third latest, but her turf form isn't as good

runners.name: Spirit Of Eagles
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over 5f at Down Royal three years ago, but she was twice second at that track last year after finishing fourth (missed break) in this; lacks a recent run this time around.
runners.comment: Twice second at Down Royal last year after finishing fourth in this; lacks a recent run

runners.name: Rough Diamond
runners.spotlight: Smart handicapper back in the day for Eddie Lynam; wasn't able to add to his tally of five wins when moving to Britain; operating at much lower level nowadays, but not showing much for present trainer who tries blinkers here.
runners.comment: Operating at much lower level now but not showing much for latest trainer; blinkers

runners.name: Skillman Ave
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f at Tipperary and second over 6f at Fairyhouse last summer when with Gavin Cromwell; very little so far for this yard and mark in freefall; few quid for him at Navan last weekend where he didn't shape too badly.
runners.comment: Won over 5f last summer; out of form, but few quid for him at Navan and shaped better

runners.name: Ukiyo
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at Cork on yielding for Gavin Cromwell last year and had several of these behind when quietly-fancied second at Navan in March; decent chance if she's as good around a turning track.
runners.comment: Winner over 6f; had several of these behind when quietly-fancied second at Navan in March

runners.name: Bel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to yard's 5f winner Nouvel Espoir, but she hasn't anything yet in four starts.
runners.comment: Half-sister to yard's 5f winner Nouvel Espoir, but she hasn't anything yet in four starts
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Star Allure
runners.spotlight: Modest performer was third in a couple of 5f maidens last year after finishing fourth (led) over C&D in August; well held in two AW runs in early 2026.
runners.comment: Modest performer was third in a couple of 5f maidens last year after C&D fourth in August

runners.name: Polar Bear
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for three years and only had two outings last term; fair fourth in race featuring several of these at Navan  (6f) in March and kept on well from long way back (missed break) when third over 5f there last weekend; likely to be strong at the finish, but the break will be crucial from gate one.
runners.comment: Likely to be strong at the finish, but the break will be crucial from gate one.

runners.name: El Fontenaro
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best form has been at 5f, including on seasonal debut last term when close third at Down Royal; this trip might just stretch her; blinkers swapped for cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Maiden's best form has been at 5f, including when close third on seasonal debut last term

runners.name: March To The Band
runners.spotlight: Back with yard after a spell in Britain; yet to place in eight starts and no real progression on AW over the winter; just second run on turf here.
runners.comment: Yet to place in eight starts and no real progression on AW over the winter

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; maiden but plenty of decent efforts over shorter trips last year and, following good run over hurdles, was fifth over 1m4f last weekend; first try at this trip.
runners.comment: Maiden but plenty of decent efforts over shorter trips; fifth over 1m4f latest; reserve

runners.name: Autocrat
runners.spotlight: Not long with Donegal yard and Donegal rider aboard; all six wins have come on AW at Dundalk, but he went close at Roscommon this time last year; best at 1m4f but has run some decent races over 2m.
runners.comment: All six wins have come on AW at Dundalk, but went close at Roscommon this time last year

runners.name: Lisnadill
runners.spotlight: Was placed twice for Andy Oliver last summer, including a narrow defeat when favourite at Naas (10.5f); after a long layoff, finished in rear on stable debut in March; markedly up in trip with new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Narrow defeat when favourite at Naas (10.5f) for previous yard; markedly up in trip

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Veteran has done a bit of everything and recorded his eighth win when scoring over 2m on AW in March; poor run at Bellewstown last time but that was only a day after being placed at Dundalk; goes well at Ballinrobe and this track isn't dissimilar.
runners.comment: Won over 2m on AW in March; goes well at Ballinrobe and this track isn't dissimilar

runners.name: Circus Act
runners.spotlight: Second runner for James Nash; dual-purpose mare has won twice over hurdles; maiden in this sphere but her fourth to You Make Me Smile in last year's renewal was one of her best Flat runs and she has a nice pull at the weights; place chance.
runners.comment: Fourth to You Make Me Smile in last year's renewal and has nice pull at the weights

runners.name: Idomything
runners.spotlight: First reserve; got the better of You Make Me Smile when winning at Bellewstown in October but well behind that rival on recent seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Beat  You Make Me Smile when winning at Bellewstown in October; well held lately; reserve

runners.name: Hobart
runners.spotlight: Fair hurdler and ran well in a couple of maidens last summer; hasn't had much Flat racing and ordinary form on the level thus far; first time in headgear.
runners.comment: Fair hurdler hasn't had much Flat racing and ordinary form on the level thus far; headgear

runners.name: Maxwell Smart
runners.spotlight: Decent performer over this kind of trip; had four seconds (three for this rider) and a win last year and he's had a spin over hurdles to warm up; ground doesn't matter to him and should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Had four seconds (three for this rider) and a win last year and this trip suits
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd/D O'Leary/J McNally

runners.name: Itsalonglongroad
runners.spotlight: Old boy has won 12 races in all under both codes, mostly from the front; C&D winner in 2024 and was placed over 1m4f last year, but he's pushing on now and tailed off over fences last time.
runners.comment: Old boy has won 12 races in all under both codes, but pushing on now and looks opposable

runners.name: You Make Me Smile
runners.spotlight: Locally-owned mare took a long time to come to hand but thrived under both codes last year, recording four wins including this race and a handicap hurdle here in October; promising run at Navan lately and headgear worn for latest Flat win is back on; crack apprentice on board too, so loads to like.
runners.comment: Won this last year and promising run at Navan lately; crack apprentice on board
runners.owner: T & R Hennessy & A Fahy Partnership

runners.name: Share The Treasure
runners.spotlight: Got a win on the board over 1m6f at Navan in August but well held afterwards, and is probably going to need this first run of the year.
runners.comment: Got a win on the board over 1m6f at Navan in August but well held after; may need the run

runners.name: Miners Cabin
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; nothing so far Flat or hurdles, but trainer always respected and homebred has staying pedigree (dam won over 2m); handicap debut.
runners.comment: Nothing so far Flat or hurdles, but trainer respected and has staying pedigree; reserve

runners.name: Just For Yuse
runners.spotlight: Modest dual-purpose performer has done little Flat racing of late; returned from nearly two years off in October and ran well when fourth in long-distance chase last month, but easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Modest dual-purpose performer has done little Flat racing of late; fair chase run lately

runners.name: Awakening
runners.spotlight: With the exception of a decent effort on debut, her maiden form has been moderate; not seen since a couple of spins over hurdles last August; tongue-tie on for handicap debut.
runners.comment: With the exception of a decent debut effort, her maiden form has been moderate; tongue-tie

runners.name: Haughty
runners.spotlight: Best form over 7f/1m, including sole win in Britain as 3yo; stable debut after layoff and not bred for this sort of test.
runners.comment: Best form over 7f/1m, stable debut after layoff and not bred for this sort of test.

runners.name: Cornmarket
runners.spotlight: Mayo contender was a revelation for his new trainer last year; won three hurdles on decent ground here, latest over 3m1f; showed nothing in Flat maidens as a youngster, but he's a different model nowadays and could be very well in; has won off similar layoff.
runners.comment: Won three hurdles here last year and could be very well handicapped on the Flat
runners.owner: John J Cullen & Michael C Barrett

runners.name: Pons Aelius
runners.spotlight: Donegal yard likes to pick up horses with back-form, often from Britain, and try to rejuvenate them; six-time winner at up to 2m but hasn't been finishing out his races in Ireland and been off a while.
runners.comment: Six-time winner at up to 2m in Britain but hasn't shown much for Donegal yard

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Beauparc
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m4f either side of being well beaten in this race last year, and been in good form over that trip on AW lately; dropping back in trip not a positive.
runners.comment: Won over 1m4f either side of being well beaten in this last year, trip may be too sharp

runners.name: God Knows
runners.spotlight: Twice second for this yard after being acquired from a claimer last summer, including a near-miss over 1m at the Curragh in October; never involved on her comeback there over 7f; up in grade and was well beaten in this two years ago; headgear.
runners.comment: Near-miss over 1m at the Curragh in October, never involved on comeback there; up in grade

runners.name: Lady Mairen
runners.spotlight: Respected connections and had smart form at two, placed twice and beaten less than 5l in Curragh Group 3; missed 3yo campaign; disappointing in a winnable maiden on her return over 1m3f at Bellewstown, but trainer has said his horses have been a bit behind schedule this year; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Smart form at two, missed 3yo campaign; disappointing in a winnable maiden on her return

runners.name: Chica Guerrera
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards trip; 1m4f winner last year and finished the season well with placed efforts in big fields at the Curragh, latterly over 1m; too keen on last month's return; would prefer it softer.
runners.comment: Versatile as regards trip and finished last season in good form; too keen on return

runners.name: Yulia
runners.spotlight: Trainer has won three of the last five renewals, all with imports making seasonal/stable debut; this one, bought for 190,000euros after two wins in France (on soft and AW), fits the bill with a tongue-tie added; has won over 1m7f, and never raced over less than 1m4f, but likely she's shown enough pace at home to come here.
runners.comment: French import has won twice over further, but likely shown enough toe at home to come here

runners.name: Narlita
runners.spotlight: Had looked fairly exposed but found more improvement last term and has carried that on with two excellent runs at the Curragh this spring, latest when third behind two progressive sorts over 1m2f; ground not being as soft as she'd like is one concern, as is a poor strike-rate for one of her ability.
runners.comment: Two excellent runs at the Curragh this spring, but would prefer softer ground

runners.name: Thrifty Of Digby
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, including over just short of this trip at Roscommon on third run of the season; fitter for return and 7lb claimed now; might be capable of more improvement this term.
runners.comment: Two wins last year, including over just short of this trip; may have more to offer

runners.name: So Must I
runners.spotlight: Sole 3yo is second runner for yard with good record in this race; got the better of better-fancied stablemate in AW rated race in February; fair run against male rivals when beaten 5l in strong 3yo handicap at Leopardstown, but she's now 3lb higher as she's out of the handicap; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: AW winner had fair run at Leopardstown and now tried in cheekpieces; 3lb wrong

runners.name: Inthesmallhours
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of 1m4f AW maiden just before Christmas 2024, but not seen since; could be on a fair mark and trainer does well here, but stiff task all the same on turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Easy winner of 1m4f AW maiden in late 2024, but not seen since;  on turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Electric Beauty
runners.spotlight: Been busy this year and was fit from AW when winning over 1m at the Curragh in March; touched off on her return there before disappointing when favourite at Gowran; fourth over this trip on AW last time after making the running,  and not sure she wants to go this far.
runners.comment: Did well over 1m at the Curragh in March; led when fourth over this trip on AW last time

runners.name: Giselles Defence
runners.spotlight: Won four times for Ian Williams on a variety of ground, all at 1m2f; nothing for this yard at big odds until mildly encouraging run at Bellewstown last time; drops in class now and has his ground, worth a market check.
runners.comment: Mildly encouraging run at Bellewstown; drops in class and has his ground, worth a look

runners.name: Metamorpheus
runners.spotlight: No success since a Punchestown maiden hurdle win (2m, heavy) three years ago; Downpatrick third in March was more encouraging but of limited appeal back on the Flat here.
runners.comment: Downpatrick hurdle third was more encouraging but of limited appeal back on the Flat

runners.name: Think About It
runners.spotlight: Maiden showed bits and pieces of form last year at 1m whne with Johnny Murtagh; , soft ground at Gowran latest probably wasn't ideal, others preferred though.
runners.comment: Soft ground at Gowran latest probably wasn't ideal; others preferred though

runners.name: Spirit Of Indalo
runners.spotlight: Very lightly-raced 4yo filly enters handicaps after three maiden runs over much shorter trips; potential improver, market may guide.
runners.comment: Potential improver upped in trip for handicap debut; market may guide

runners.name: Cuckaloo Hill
runners.spotlight: Placed in good-ground handicaps over this trip at Fairyhouse and Naas last summer for Paul Nolan; three later runs for this yard were poor and reappears here in first-time tongue-tie; ground should suit and handicapper giving him a chance.
runners.comment: Needs to improve on most recent form; first-time tongue-tie, ground should suit

runners.name: Copper Craft
runners.spotlight: Low-grade handicapper but in good form on the AW of late, most recently 13-8 fav when only denied second late on over this trip 10 days ago; same mark here, should go well.
runners.comment: Recent AW third over this trip makes him a major contender off same mark

runners.name: Cleopatra's Needle
runners.spotlight: Sole Flat win (from 30 starts) in 1m2f Navan handicap in 2024; better hurdler, winning at last year's Galway festival and ran a cracker on return to finish second in 1m Leopardstown Flat handicap last month; race may have come too soon when never counting over hurdles six days later, but has to be respected back on the level.
runners.comment: Leopardstown comeback run makes her a big player, despite hurdles flop six days later

runners.name: She's Ideal
runners.spotlight: Comeback run at Gowran last month was promising (staying-on fourth over 1m); first attempt at this longer trip, but handicapped to go well if she stays.
runners.comment: Gowran comeback run promising; should go close if staying this longer trip

runners.name: Sarangpur
runners.spotlight: Career-best over just shy of this trip on handicap debut at Gowran last month when up with much of pace in finishing third on soft ground; quicker ground and sharper track could enable him to see this out, so definite player.
runners.comment: Gowran third; quicker ground and sharper track could enable him to see this out

runners.name: Serengeti Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Hasn't added to Wolverhampton 7f maiden win in 2023; touched off at Gowran (heavy) this time last year, but form since none too encouraging and takes a big step up in trip here.
runners.comment: Recent form since none too encouraging and takes a big step up in trip here

runners.name: Keilah
runners.spotlight: Sole win for Willie McCreery came over 1m1f at Punchestown in 2024; some decent efforts in defeat last season and picked up for 5,000euros in November; debuts for local yard in first-time tongue-tie and market could be interesting.
runners.comment: 5,000euros buy in November; debuts for local yard in first-time tongue-tie

runners.name: Haeret In Pectore
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f when trained in Poland; showed a glimmer at Dundalk in January (over this trip) but didn't back it up back there later that month and off since; 2lb wrong here.
runners.comment: Penultimate AW run not without promise; 2lb wrong here

runners.name: Angel Harry
runners.spotlight: Maiden runs over various trips haven't amounted to much and ran no sort of race upped to 1m4f for handicap debut when last seen in September; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Ran no sort of race upped to 1m4f for handicap debut when last seen; hard to fancy

runners.name: Creative Dancer
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden under both codes, well held over hurdles when last seen in January; 5lb out of handicap here.
runners.comment: Poor maiden under both codes, well held over hurdles when last seen; 5lb wrong

runners.name: Pliny
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 7f, including when second in AW claimer in March, but most exposed of these and not sure what trip he wants; well held over this distance and 1m4f last twice; down 16lb from initial rating.
runners.comment: Placed twice over 7f, but most exposed of these and not sure what trip he wants

runners.name: Down The Glen
runners.spotlight: Homebred nursery winner over 7f on heavy ground and staying-on sixth over 1m (soft) in higher-grade Curragh contest on recent return to action; dam won over 1m and he should stay, but easy ground is said to be important to him.
runners.comment: Nursery winner over 7f and staying-on sixth over 1m in higher-grade Curragh contest lately

runners.name: Sands Castle
runners.spotlight: Much-improved fourth at huge price in 1m maiden at Gowran, staying on steadily to finish only 2l off 83-rated third; well handicapped in theory on that line of form.
runners.comment: Much-improved fourth at huge price in 1m maiden at Gowran, only 2l off 83-rated third

runners.name: Misty Cove
runners.spotlight: Two fair AW runs, and although form of Kempton third over 1m3f in January hasn't worked out, she had a couple of subsequent winners behind when fourth at Dundalk; first handicap.
runners.comment: Two fair AW runs, although form of Kempton third over 1m3f in January hasn't worked out

runners.name: Nephin Mountain
runners.spotlight: Two good runs in 7f handicaps at Gowran and stayed on steadily last time when beaten 7l by well-handicapped  winner; sire not really an influence for stamina, but he might improve for this longer trip, and yard has good record in the race.
runners.comment: Two good runs in 7f handicaps at Gowran and stayed on steadily last time; trip may help

runners.name: Music And Song
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners from 7f to 1m6f, several of them useful; huge prices when out the back in AW maidens; tried in a hood last time, now goes with cheekpieces; 3lb wrong.
runners.comment: Huge prices when out the back in AW maidens; headgear changed for handicap bow; 3lb wrong

runners.name: The Wee Man
runners.spotlight: Cheap yearling ran okay at Galway on sole start at two; quiet in two Curragh maidens this term; very stoutly bred.
runners.comment: Cheap yearling ran okay on sole start at two; quiet in two Curragh maidens this term

runners.name: Kart Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Cost E46,000 as a yearling and AW debut wasn't without merit; some stamina further back in pedigree and talented apprentice rides for the first time; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Cost E46,000 as a yearling and AW debut wasn't without merit; some stamina in pedigree

runners.name: Dawson Street
runners.spotlight: Beaten 4l on handicap debut over 1m at Dundalk in November; hampered early on when not as good back there the following month; bred to get this trip.
runners.comment: Beaten 4l on handicap debut over 1m at Dundalk, not as good there back in December
runners.owner: Roderick Ryan & Mrs John M Weld

runners.name: Augusta Rock
runners.spotlight: Stable is dynamite in 3yo handicaps, even when maiden form is moderate, as is the case with this colt; close relation to 1m2f winner; headgear added for handicap debut and starts off from a very workable mark.
runners.comment: Stable are dynamite in 3yo handicaps, even when maiden form is moderate; headgear added

runners.name: Wipeawayyourtears
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in 7f/1m maidens last year; long layoff and not really bred to appreciate this longer trip; small yard amongst the winners lately.
runners.comment: Ran okay in 7f/1m maidens last year; not really bred to appreciate this longer trip

races.race_name: Celebrating Our Shared Champion Spirit Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Triumphant Warrior
runners.spotlight: 26-race maiden but on his final turf run he was a close fourth to Majestic Delight; 5lb lower than that day and Nichola Yuen takes off 10lb, so by no means dismissed.
runners.comment: 26-race maiden but on his final turf run he was a close fourth to Majestic Delight
runners.owner: Bernard Man, Stephen Man Shiu Fai & Gerard Man

runners.name: Circuit Victory
runners.spotlight: The blinkers worked well initially but 0-9 since that breakthrough win, last time only fifth behind Majestic Delight; has since won a trial.
runners.comment: The blinkers worked well initially but 0-9 since that breakthrough win

runners.name: Autumn Vibes
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped and returned to form with a third at Happy Valley in February, since when he's won a trial; reduced mark gets him in at this level for a first time and Zac Purton is booked for yard debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Reduced mark gets him in at this level for a first time and Purton is booked

runners.name: Diriya
runners.spotlight: It was on the dirt when disappointing in his first Class 5 contest a month ago but overall profile isn't that convincing.
runners.comment: It was on the dirt when disappointing in his first Class 5 contest a month ago

runners.name: Supreme Winner
runners.spotlight: Out of the weights and had overweight when beating Always Fluke and others in here at this track a month ago, so in effect he's just 5lb higher now; enters the equation.
runners.comment: Beat a few of these here latest and effectively up 5lb, which is not the end of the world

runners.name: Bingo Babe
runners.spotlight: Reserve; now 0-20 and opposable on the back of two ordinary runs for new yard.
runners.comment: Reserve; now 0-20 and opposable on the back of two ordinary runs for new yard
runners.owner: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho

runners.name: Majestic Delight
runners.spotlight: Went up 6lb for his C&D win in March and off this figure he was behind a few of these back here a month ago; that was out of the same box as today.
runners.comment: After a 6lb rise he was behind a few of these back here a month ago

runners.name: Call To Command
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden but only a length away three runs ago and feasible excuses since on account of the draw and trip (7f); from stall 1 he's no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Maiden but feasible excuses since third in March on account of the draw and trip (7f)

runners.name: Basic Instinct
runners.spotlight: Perhaps failed to stay 7f last time but has yet to hit the frame in Hong Kong and unwise to be making excuses for him.
runners.comment: Perhaps failed to stay 7f last time but has yet to hit the frame in Hong Kong
runners.owner: 22/23 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: King Alloy
runners.spotlight: Running well, and from the front he wasn't far behind Supreme Winner and Always Fluke here last time; stall 13 is a major blow, though.
runners.comment: Running well but when front running in his game, stall 13 is a major blow
runners.owner: Lun Ka Leung & Yeung Hiu Yu

runners.name: Country Dancer
runners.spotlight: Reserve; C&D winner on a pretty good mark and returning to 6f is a positive; each-way chance if getting in.
runners.comment: Reserve; C&D winner on a pretty good mark and returning to 6f is a positive

runners.name: Always Fluke
runners.spotlight: Only 1-20 but back in a Class 5 he pushed Supreme Winner close over C&D a month ago despite a troubled run; would have been tempting but for stall 12.
runners.comment: Excellent effort last time and would have been tempting but for stall 12

runners.name: Good Fortune
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when twice fifth over C&D in March and when back in a Class 5 last time that was over 7f; has races in him off this mark but could be better drawn.
runners.comment: 7f too far latest; has races in him off this mark but could be better drawn

runners.name: Glory Cloud
runners.spotlight: Has only won on dirt but has threatened on turf; a greater concern would be his current form.
runners.comment: Has only won on dirt but has threatened on turf; a greater concern would be current form

runners.name: Happy Alliance
runners.spotlight: Poorly drawn dropped into a Class 5 at Happy Valley and again unlucky in that respect; not had lots of racing but likely one for another day.
runners.comment: Poorly drawn dropped into a Class 5 at Happy Valley and again unlucky in that respect

runners.name: Gimme Five
runners.spotlight: Won at this level on dirt here last summer but has lost his way badly and can only be watched for now, despite finishing second in a recent trial.
runners.comment: Won at this level on dirt here last summer but has lost his way badly

runners.name: Malpensa
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; bang there in two 6f handicaps here before a lesser show on dirt when an eyeshield was added (now removed); on those previous efforts he looks a player.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; bang there in two 6f handicaps here before a lesser show on dirt
runners.owner: 24/25 Chris So Wai Yin Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Bullish Pride
runners.spotlight: All six runs have been here (5f/6f) and he hasn't been shaping like a winner waiting to happen.
runners.comment: All six runs here (5f/6f); hasn't been shaping like a winner waiting to happen
runners.owner: Wong Wing Keung & Cheung Ka Ho

runners.name: Same To You
runners.spotlight: Not overly consistent but last couple of efforts at Happy Valley can be upgraded and he was closing late on; stall 12 is a stumbling block.
runners.comment: Last couple of efforts at Happy Valley can be upgraded and he was closing late on
runners.owner: Colin Cheung Kwok Lam, Amos Cheung Sai Keung & Gary H K Yau

runners.name: Master Lucky
runners.spotlight: Made successful debut over 5f here in February and has shown enough in his four starts since (including at 6f) to suggest he can win races off this sort of mark; makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Debut winner over 5f; has been showing enough to believe he can win again

runners.name: Leading Dragon
runners.spotlight: Has a lot of early speed and he hung around for a close second over C&D 21 days ago under Nichola Yuen; might well get to the front from stall 5 and could prove tough to pass.
runners.comment: Might well get to the front from stall 5 and could prove tough to pass

runners.name: King Oberon
runners.spotlight: Reserve; C&D winner last summer and twice successful at Happy Valley since; hasn't shone this year, though.
runners.comment: A three-time winner but hasn't shone this year

runners.name: Top Throne
runners.spotlight: C&D winner a year ago; continues to drop in the weights and ran pretty well to be fourth here three weeks ago when not far behind Leading Dragon; there were no excuses but he's drawn 1 and has Zac Purton up top.
runners.comment: No excuses last time but he's drawn 1 and has Purton up top

runners.name: Super Love
runners.spotlight: Both wins here, over 6f and 7f; however, probably needs further now and was well beaten back at 6f the last time.
runners.comment: Probably needs further now and was well beaten back at 6f the last time

runners.name: Power Patch
runners.spotlight: No better than 11th in his three official runs and remains on a harsh mark by the looks; placed in some recent trials.
runners.comment: No better than 11th in his three official runs and remains on a harsh mark by the looks

runners.name: Magic Super
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has no form claims and remains on an unrealistic mark; new trainer.
runners.comment: Reserve; has no form claims and remains on an unrealistic mark; new trainer

runners.name: Lightness Of Being
runners.spotlight: Won in Ireland but it's been a struggle in Hong Kong and for now there's no apparent light at the end of the tunnel.
runners.comment: Struggled in Hong Kong and for now there's no apparent light at the end of the tunnel

runners.name: Oneshot
runners.spotlight: Some ability in trials and again when a never-dangerous fifth over 5f here two weeks ago; could improve for this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Some ability in trials and again when a never-dangerous fifth over 5f here two weeks ago
runners.owner: Anthony Chan Wai Choi, Wong Tsz Wa & Kwok Ying Lung

runners.name: Casa Buddy
runners.spotlight: Plenty of defeats in trials and was a big price when down the field on official debut here over 5f; same mark.
runners.comment: A big price when down the field on official debut here over 5f; same mark

runners.name: Chiu Chow Golf
runners.spotlight: Had a few chances in trials this year, twice finishing runner-up; has to prove himself worthy of this opening mark.
runners.comment: Twice second in trials but has to prove himself worthy of this opening mark

runners.name: Happy Smile
runners.spotlight: A mixed bag in trials and wouldn't be the obvious answer on this official debut.
runners.comment: A mixed bag in trials and wouldn't be the obvious answer on this official debut
runners.owner: Chan Tung, Lo Wing Man Et Al

runners.name: Virtus Glory
runners.spotlight: Has won a couple of trials this year but a wide draw asks plenty of him on official debut.
runners.comment: Has won a couple of trials this year but a wide draw asks plenty of him on official debut

runners.name: Panda Legend
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate but wasn't far behind He Was Me two runs ago and did well to be fifth behind the same horse (and others in here) when drawn very wide last time; another to consider.
runners.comment: Another to consider after running so well from a wicked draw here last time

runners.name: Winning Machine
runners.spotlight: Three positive runs since joining current yard, last time finishing hot on the heels of He Was Me and Yee Cheong Raider over C&D; had no excuses but he's drawn in 5 and Ellis Wong is back aboard claiming his 3lb.
runners.comment: Close up behind two of these last time; Ellis Wong is back aboard claiming his 3lb
runners.owner: Sammy Ma Hon Kit/Chan Sin Shui/Exec Of Est Of J C T Cheung

runners.name: The Way We Win
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden whose sole placing was last October; opposable.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden whose sole placing was last October; opposable

runners.name: Power Summit
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden and hasn't remotely threatened to put that right in last three races.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden and hasn't remotely threatened to put that right in last three races

runners.name: Mazing Grace
runners.spotlight: Only sixth in a recent trial and hadn't been pulling up any trees in 7f-1m1f handicaps here.
runners.comment: Only sixth in recent trial and hadn't been pulling up any trees in 7f-1m1f handicaps here

runners.name: Frantanck
runners.spotlight: Way better than this grade in his prime and last two efforts in Class 4s were respectable; would have been tempting under Nichola Yuen had he not been drawn so wide (often front runs).
runners.comment: Would have been very tempting under Nichola Yuen had he not been drawn so wide

runners.name: He Was Me
runners.spotlight: Raised 11lb for winning two of his last three races and he had the run of things up front from a good draw when repelling a few of these last time; will do well to repeat the trick from stall 13.
runners.comment: Beat a few of these last time but he did have the run of things from a good draw

runners.name: Rewarding Twinkle
runners.spotlight: 0-17 but in form and has been getting behind in 6f handicaps before running on late; returning to 7f has to help; each-way claims.
runners.comment: 0-17 but in form and has been getting behind in 6f handicaps before running on late

runners.name: Superb Guy
runners.spotlight: Reserve; hasn't been dropping lucky with the draws but he is a 13-race maiden; others safer.
runners.comment: Reserve; hasn't been dropping lucky with the draws but he is a 13-race maiden

runners.name: View All Things
runners.spotlight: Beaten only 2.5l from well off the pace over C&D last month and that was after a lengthy break; has since finished second in a trial under Zac Purton; definite chance from stall 3.
runners.comment: Good run after a lengthy break and has since finished second in a trial under Zac Purton

runners.name: Viva Taste
runners.spotlight: Never progressed in Class 4s but despite starting slowly he was only 2l off the winner when dropped into a Class 5 here two weeks ago; however, that was over 1m1f and he has raced over even further, so there are questions to be answered back at 7f.
runners.comment: Pleasing run last time but over 1m1f and there are questions to be answered back at 7f
runners.owner: Jimmy Chung Kwok Man & Dominic Chung Won Lok

runners.name: Winning Diamond
runners.spotlight: Wasn't showing a lot in Class 4s but ran better at this level when closing for fourth behind He Was Me, Yee Cheong Raider and Winning Machine over C&D three weeks ago; however, he was in stall 3 that day and now exits 12.
runners.comment: Chance on last run but he was in stall 3 that day and now exits 12

runners.name: Yee Cheong Raider
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden but deserves a break and did well to get a prominent position from stall 12 when ultimately edged out by He Was Me here last month; only 2lb higher, better drawn this time and should have every chance.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden but deserves a break; again luckless here 21 days ago
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Chun Lung

runners.name: Winning Cigar
runners.spotlight: Reserve; slow start was no help when behind a host of these here three weeks ago; still opposable.
runners.comment: Reserve; slow start was no help when behind a host of these here three weeks ago

runners.name: Soaring Bronco
runners.spotlight: First and second in recent 1m handicaps at Happy Valley, latterly from a wide draw; still on a realistic mark but it's been a long time since he last competed over 7f.
runners.comment: Arrives here in a good run of form but a long time since he last competed over 7f
runners.owner: Simon Yung Yun Sang, Tang Ka Nin Et Al

runners.name: Gale Saga
runners.spotlight: Both wins over C&D and has run some respectable races during his losing sequence that now stretches beyond a year; however, was well behind some of these last time and he's moved trainers; well held in recent trials.
runners.comment: Long losing run; well behind some of these last time and he's moved trainers

races.race_name: Queen Mother Memorial Cup (Group 3 Handicap) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Nautical Force
runners.spotlight: Useful at up to 1m4f when when trained in Ireland and came from off the pace to make winning Hong Kong debut here (1m1f) last month; back up in trip today and needs to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Scored over 1m1f on recent Hong Kong debut and this longer trip will suit

runners.name: Stunning Peach
runners.spotlight: Took form to new level when winning twice in February/March (1m/1m1f); subsequent Group 2 defeat is not viewed too harshly but he was only ninth behind Nautical Force when back in a handicap (1m1f) two weeks ago; needs to kick on again over this new trip.
runners.comment: Dual winner this year but only ninth behind Nautical Force here last month

runners.name: Winning Wing
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner over 1m2f this season (unraced over further); ran about as well as could have been expected when third of six (no match for winner) in Group 1 over that trip in March but could only manage eighth behind Nautical Force when dropped back to 1m1f two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Triple 1m2f course winner this season; safely held when dropped back to 1m1f last time

runners.name: Romantic Thor
runners.spotlight: In good form over 1m on dirt in recent months, making the frame on his last three appearances; should be fine back on turf and stays at least 1m2f but is 6lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: In good form on dirt lately; should be fine back on turf but is 6lb wrong
runners.owner: Peter Lau Pak Fai & Suki Tang Xianfang

runners.name: Fortune Boy
runners.spotlight: Made the frame three consecutive times in February/March (1m1f-1m2f) but was a shade disappointing when last in action and competes from 4lb out of the weights today; needs to find something extra over the new trip.
runners.comment: Largely in good form this season but competes from 4lb out of the weights today
runners.owner: Ricky Wong Kin Chat & Helen Leung Yee Wan

runners.name: Joy Of Spring
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over 1m1f; placed on last two appearances, more recently when third over that trip behind Nautical Force here a fortnight ago; has another big role to play if this extra 3f suits.
runners.comment: Ran well when third over 1m1f here last month; goes beyond that trip for first time
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung

runners.name: Gentlemen Legacy
runners.spotlight: Improving 5yo who won consecutive 1m2f races here in February/March and again ran well when fourth in 1m1f contest won by Nautical Force a fortnight ago; tries a new trip today; respected.
runners.comment: Dual 1m2f winner this year and again ran well over 1m1f last month; respected

races.race_name: Beas River Country Club Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Kingly Demeanor
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D off 2lb higher at end of last season and was runner-up over today's trip at Happy Valley on his last two outings; bold bid from the front likely.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice at Happy Valley in recent weeks; bold bid from the front likely

runners.name: Volcanic Spark
runners.spotlight: Without a win since 2024 but lost out only narrowly to Charity Together here (1m2f) in March and has now been placed on three of his last four appearances; can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Placed on three of his last four appearances and can make presence felt again

runners.name: Kolachi
runners.spotlight: 0-15 in Hong Kong; kept on for very respectable fourth at Happy Valley (1m) in March but did not get competitive over that C&D last month; needs only to drop another 1lb to be eligible for a drop in grade.
runners.comment: 0-15 in Hong Kong; needs only to drop another 1lb to be eligible for drop in grade

runners.name: Fortune Kingo
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; placed over 7f in December but has struggled over 1m+ in recent months; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden who has struggled in recent months

runners.name: Mr Cool
runners.spotlight: In the frame all five starts this year, most recently when third over 1m here last month; obvious contender if stamina holds out over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: In the frame all five starts this year (6f-1m); tries a new trip today

runners.name: Joyful Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Dug deep to win Class 5 event at Happy Valley in March and again ran well when third in this higher grade over that C&D on Wednesday; might find others better handicapped here, though.
runners.comment: Good third at Happy Valley (1m3f) on Wednesday; might find others better treated here
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Silas S S Yang & Michelle H Y Yang

runners.name: Yiu Cheung Victory
runners.spotlight: Two wins this season, the latter over 1m2f here in January; rather inconsistent since but back on a workable mark now; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Inconsistent since 1m2f win in January but back on a workable mark now

runners.name: Amazing Gaze
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after ten starts but has been pretty consistent; shaped as though this move back up in trip will suit when keeping-on sixth over 1m last month; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: 0-10 but pretty consistent; each-way claims at least on this move back up in trip

runners.name: Charity Together
runners.spotlight: Consistent 4yo who kept on well from Volcanic Spark for narrow 1m2f course win in March, opening his Hong Kong account at the 11th attempt; might be vulnerable after recent 6lb rise but ought to give another good account.
runners.comment: Consistent 4yo who kept on well for narrow 1m2f win in March; up 6lb

runners.name: Kyrus Treasure
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; outran 75-1 odds when fourth here (1m) on New Year's Day but struggled on both subsequent outings; upped in trip today.
runners.comment: Struggled on last two outings and is now 0-6

runners.name: Flying Boom
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when scrambling home by a short head here (1m) in February, his sixth start; placed off today's mark on next outing but needs to rebound from a quiet run last month; moves up in trip.
runners.comment: Narrow 1m winner in February; needs to rebound from last month's quiet run
runners.owner: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan

runners.name: Storm Runner
runners.spotlight: Ran as though something was amiss last time but won consecutive 1m1f races (one here) in February/March and has good 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen enlisted today; big player if back on song.
runners.comment: Not himself last time; won previous two races; good 10lb claimer enlisted

runners.name: Vermilion Tempest
runners.spotlight: Never really looked dangerous when midfield over 1m at Happy Valley last month and has now been unplaced all seven Hong Kong starts; back up in trip today; improvement required.
runners.comment: Never dangerous when midfield last month; now 0-7 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Grand Turbo
runners.spotlight: A down-the-field outsider on all three starts (7f-1m); can't be recommended.
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on all three starts (7f-1m)

runners.name: Beauty Eternal
runners.spotlight: Smart in his prime but on the downgrade now and yet to be placed after nine starts this season; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Smart in his prime but yet to be placed after nine starts this season; revival needed

runners.name: Packing Angel
