runners.spotlight: Close third in last season's Hong Kong Derby but didn't reappear until January and has been rather disappointing this term, albeit he didn't run badly when fifth behind Max Que over C&D last month.
runners.comment: Placed in last season's Hong Kong Derby but rather disappointing this year

runners.name: Beauty Joy
runners.spotlight: Four-time Group winner, most recently over 1m1f here last May; well below best since pleasing seasonal debut in October but sure to appreciate this ease in grade and has been given a good chance by the handicapper.
runners.comment: On the downgrade now but sure to appreciate this ease in grade
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Pocketing
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Group 2 in Australia last April; raced too freely and was unable to justify prominence in the market when only seventh of nine in C&D event won by Max Que on Hong Kong debut in March but ought to be capable of better.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner in Australia; HK debut was underwhelming but he can improve

runners.name: Sky Jewellery
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who was an emphatic 7f course winner last month and is now 4-6; up in grade today but this return to 1m will suit and it's hard to gauge the limit of his ability; makes obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Emphatic 7f course winner last month and now 4-6; makes obvious appeal

runners.name: Kaholo Angel
runners.spotlight: Now only 2lb higher than when dead-heating at Happy Valley in December, his first attempt at 1m, but his form since the turn of the year has been uninspiring.
runners.comment: Dead-heated over 1m in December but this year's form is uninspiring
runners.owner: Ho Garrick Sai Man & Tam Wing Yee

runners.name: Max Que
runners.spotlight: Consistent and improving 5yo who has collected four wins this season, the latest over C&D in March; another 6lb higher today but seems very likely to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Consistent and improving 5yo who has collected four wins this season; respected
runners.owner: Ricky Ng Yu Chiu & To Ngo Fung

runners.name: Californiatotality
runners.spotlight: Went very close off today's mark at Happy Valley (1m1f) in October but returned from a break with three lesser efforts in February/March; needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Went close off this mark in October; back from break with three lesser efforts in 2026

runners.name: Gorgeous Win
runners.spotlight: Gained this season's win over 6f on dirt; also suited by turf and is unexposed over 1m but recent efforts have been disappointing; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Gained this season's win over 6f on dirt; recent efforts have been disappointing

runners.name: Stormy Grove
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who won the Classic Cup here (1m1f) in March and was placed in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) later that month; drops back in trip for this handicap return; needs to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Progressive 4yo; back in a handicap after finishing third in Hong Kong Derby in March
runners.owner: Lily Chan Li Lei & Marc Chan

runners.name: Helene Feeling
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two wins at Happy Valley (1m1f/1m) but has not been in quite the same form lately and might still be a shade too high in the weights.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two wins (1m1f/1m) but not quite in same form lately

runners.name: King Of Fighters
runners.spotlight: Left disappointing stable debut behind when close second over 6f here in March but didn't fire over that trip last month and it's hard to now what to expect from him on this return to 7f; can't grumble about his draw, though.
runners.comment: Second over 6f two starts ago but other two stable runs were disappointing

runners.name: Embraces
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season but his last three runs over 1m were very respectable and he has slipped to an attractive mark; will be dangerous if combination of drop back in trip and refitted cheekpieces works for him.
runners.comment: On good mark; drops back in trip with cheekpieces refitted; interesting
runners.owner: Thomas Chung Yat Fan & Joe Ho Pui Lung

runners.name: Baby Sakura
runners.spotlight: Scored over 6f here on debut in February and was placed over that trip on both subsequent appearances; up in grade for this first attempt at 7f but probably still has potential; must be considered.
runners.comment: Made good start to career over 6f; up in grade for first 7f attempt; one to consider
runners.owner: Chan Tak Wa & Ip Wai Yuen

runners.name: The Boom Box
runners.spotlight: Now 1lb lower than when winning over 1m in November but needs to better his recent efforts and this drop back to 7f is probably not ideal; drawn on the outside; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Back on a good mark but has wide draw and would appeal more over 1m

runners.name: Charming Legend
runners.spotlight: Regressive 5yo who finished last over C&D last month and is now 0-3 for his new stable; needs first-time blinkers to help him stop the rot.
runners.comment: Regressive 5yo who finished last over C&D last month; needs to stop the rot
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Beauty Crescent
runners.spotlight: Just held on for C&D win off 2lb lower in September but looked out of sorts in early part of this year; returns from short break with a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Looked out of sorts in early part of this year; returns from short break

runners.name: Ka Ying Attack
runners.spotlight: Consistent 4yo who was placed over 6f on his last two outings and is also suited by 7f; drawn wide today but probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Consistent over 6f/7f this season; drawn wide today but won't be far away

runners.name: Red Maestro
runners.spotlight: Impressed on debut in Ireland in early 2025 (1m, Polytrack) and, following a long layoff, kept on for fifth on Hong Kong debut here (6f) in March; ought to be suited by this move back up in trip; contender if that recent outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Made promising HK debut over 6f and this move back up in trip ought to suit
runners.owner: The Hon Ronald Arculli GBM GBS JP & Johanna K J Arculli BBS

runners.name: Yee Cheong Glory
runners.spotlight: Won in good style over 6f here in March and has held his own in this higher grade twice since; could plausibly have untapped potential over 7f but wide draw reduces his appeal.
runners.comment: In good form over 6f; unexposed over 7f but wide draw reduces his appeal
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Yu So Fong

runners.name: Jumbo Steps
runners.spotlight: Reserve; unable to land a significant blow when a 61-1 outsider for Hong Kong debut here (6f) in March.
runners.comment: Unable to land a significant blow when a 61-1 outsider for Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Exceed The Limit
runners.spotlight: Smart when based in New Zealand; launched Hong Kong career with two encouraging 6f runs but was a shade disappointing over C&D last month; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Began HK career with two encouraging 6f runs; shade disappointing over C&D since

runners.name: Pi Legend
runners.spotlight: Made all to open Hong Kong account on dirt here (6f) last month, his sixth local start; flopped when warm favourite to follow up over that C&D 18 days later but his Australian form confirms this return to 7f on turf will suit.
runners.comment: 6f dirt winner here last month; Australian form confirms 7f on turf will also suit

runners.name: Quantum Legend
runners.spotlight: 6f winner on debut in New Zealand last year; disappointing favourite over that trip on last month's Hong Kong debut but Zac Purton keeps the ride for this first 7f attempt; no surprise if he gets much closer to the target.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite on HK debut (6f) but Zac Purton keeps the ride

runners.name: Fortune Link
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo who held on by a nose to make winning Hong Kong debut over C&D last month, doing well to overcome stall 12; Nichola Yuen again claims 10lb today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Held on by a nose to make winning Hong Kong debut over C&D last month; shortlisted
runners.owner: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen

runners.name: Riding Together
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner from the front this season; safely held off this career-high mark on last two outings, though, and not well drawn today.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season; safely held off this career-high mark lately

runners.name: Rising From Ashes
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed over 6f on seasonal debut in September but out of form in recent months and now eligible for a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Out of form in recent months and now eligible for a drop in grade

runners.name: Almighty Lightning
runners.spotlight: Won comfortably over 5f here on debut in February and last month's second over that trip was also very promising; up in grade for first attempt at 6f but could still have a lot more to offer; sports new blinkers.
runners.comment: Made bright start to career over 5f; up in grade for first 6f attempt

runners.name: My Mars
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 3yo who led close home to pip Turquoise Velocity over C&D on last month's Hong Kong debut; effectively 4lb worse off with him today but is drawn on the inside and has plenty of scope to build on that performance.
runners.comment: Led close home to pip Turquoise Velocity over C&D on recent Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Thousand Spirit
runners.spotlight: Gamely made all over C&D last month, his 11th Hong Kong start, and probably bettered that performance when second in this higher grade a fortnight later; evidently in good nick but this race is competitive.
runners.comment: Second in this higher grade since last month's C&D win; evidently in good nick

runners.name: Young Emperor
runners.spotlight: Stayed on for last-gasp C&D win in January and has made the frame over C&D on his last three appearances; remains on a fairly tough mark but can threaten if he gets a good pace to aim at.
runners.comment: In frame over C&D on last three starts; can threaten if he gets a good pace to chase

runners.name: Crossborderpegasus
runners.spotlight: 3-5 last season (all wins over C&D), and has performed well from double-digit stalls on both appearances this term; not yet fully exposed but his run of poor luck on the draw front has continued.
runners.comment: Ran well on both outings this season but his run of poor luck draw-wise continues

runners.name: Turquoise Velocity
runners.spotlight: 3-4 over 5f this season, and lost out only by a short head to My Mars over C&D when upped in trip last month; better drawn today and probably still has potential over 6f; good chance.
runners.comment: 3-4 over 5f; went very close when upped to 6f last month; still has potential

runners.name: Do You Just
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with clearcut C&D win last month; went up 7lb for that but further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Off the mark in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with clearcut C&D win last month

runners.name: Magnemite
runners.spotlight: Always behind when a 69-1 outsider for last month's Hong Kong debut over C&D.
runners.comment: Always behind when a 69-1 outsider for last month's Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Tactical Horizons
runners.spotlight: Made low-key Hong Kong debut at triple-digit odds here (5f) last month and has wide draw today.
runners.comment: Made low-key Hong Kong debut at triple-digit odds here (5f) last month
runners.owner: Elvis Kwok Kam Chuen, Winkle Lee Wing Ho Et Al

runners.name: Fiery Steed
runners.spotlight: Reserve; struggled to get competitive on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/6f).
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/6f)

runners.name: Majestic Valour
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who made all for clearcut C&D win in January and is now 2-3; has rise in grade and wide draw to overcome on first run since but it's hard to gauge the limit of his ability.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D in January and is now 2-3; up in grade and drawn wide today
runners.owner: Richard Yue Wah Chuk & David Pao

runners.name: Lady's Love
runners.spotlight: Made quite encouraging Hong Kong debut when fifth over C&D in February but his 7f run in March was a backward step; others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on HK debut but next run was a backward step
runners.owner: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate

runners.name: Giant Spirit
runners.spotlight: Reserve; well down the field on all three Hong Kong starts (5f-6f); can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Well down the field on all three Hong Kong starts (5f-6f)
runners.owner: Dr V Chan Chi Tao, John Tang Lam Chek & Uri Yau Wing Hay

runners.name: Smart Golf
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who made virtually all over C&D in March and is now 3-5; could still have more to offer but stall 14 makes things awkward today.
runners.comment: Improving front-runner with 3-5 strike-rate; stall 14 makes things awkward, though

runners.name: Winning Buddies
runners.spotlight: Well down the field over C&D when a big-priced outsider for Hong Kong debut in January.
runners.comment: Well down the field over C&D when a big-priced outsider for Hong Kong debut
runners.owner: 24/25 Jimmy K H Ting Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Scorching Legend
runners.spotlight: 1-7 when based in Australia, the win gained over an extended 6f in November 2024; makes Hong Kong debut after 11-month absence; best watched.
runners.comment: 1-7 in Australia; makes Hong Kong debut after 11-month absence; best watched

runners.name: West Wind Blows
runners.spotlight: Two-time Group 3 winner here over shorter, who has only finished outside the first three four times and heads into this having matched his career-best behind the mighty Calandagan in March in the Group 1 Sheema Classic (1m4f, good); leading claims here significantly down in grade.
runners.comment: Matched his career-best last time and hard to beat in that sort of form

runners.name: Map Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Group 2 winner and Group 1-placed over 1m2f earlier in his career at this track but hasn't really built on that since, including over this trip the last two times having been gelded in between; not easy to recommend on that basis but owner's retained rider seemingly prefers him to Best Secret, so a return to form couldn't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Not at best last two times but James Doyle rides him instead of Best Secret

runners.name: Oracle
runners.spotlight: Supplemented for this; another lightly raced sort in the field, who holds the distinction of being odds on to beat top Group 1 performer Daryz as a 3yo in a conditions event here; ran okay on only previous try at this trip and improved on the figures last time when fifth, beaten only 1.5l and ahead of Best Secret, so couldn't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Supplemented for this; good run last time and may do better still

runners.name: Best Secret
runners.spotlight: Not had much racing for his age and only been over this trip once before, but that was a good effort in beating the well-regarded Gethin in a Listed event; arrives unexposed over 1m4f and not too far off his best on his recent return to action over 1m2f under this rider on first venture into Group company, but the owner's retained jockey appears to have chosen Map Of Stars.
runners.comment: Unexposed at the trip and ran well last time, but Doyle prefers Map Of Stars seemingly

runners.name: Chestnut Rocket
runners.spotlight: Smart performer at his best and was just below his peak a month and a half ago in a 1m4f Listed event at Saint-Cloud on very soft; should be okay on quicker ground and fully respected, but this is a strong race for the grade and he'll need extra.
runners.comment: Not far off his best last time so respected for all that more is needed

runners.name: Euboa
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form, with trip and ground likely to suit, but hasn't won a Listed race yet let alone a Group race, which suggests she has plenty on even with the 4lb sex allowance; hard task.
runners.comment: Hasn't won a Listed race yet let alone a Group race; hard task

races.race_name: Oliver Brown Pretty Polly Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Esna
runners.spotlight: All three starts as 2yo were on soft; green in front in 7f novice win at Sandown against a notable rival; about 2l fourth of eight when an outsider for 1m Group 1 Marcel Boussac at Longchamp, keeping on well while never able to threaten seriously the lead; fair chance she'll stay 1m2f, judged on that, even if pedigree (sire sprinter) casts doubts on her Oaks entry; clear top on form.
runners.comment: Clear top on form after her Group 1 Marcel Boussac fourth; fair chance she'll stay 1m2f

runners.name: Jennifer Jane
runners.spotlight: Made all in a maiden at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on second start and almost all in Listed race at Sandown (7f, good) later last summer; her season ended, however, with last of seven in Group 3 at Goodwood (7f, good to firm) in August; good chance she will stay 1m2f; needs to resume progress.
runners.comment: Good chance she will stay 1m2f; needs to resume progress after last in a Group 3 in August

runners.name: Sacred Ground
runners.spotlight: By Kingman out of Oaks winner Anapurna; favourite for both starts as 2yo, overcoming greenness to win a five-runner novice at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) and every chance through the final 3f when second of ten in Listed race at Newmarket (1m, good to soft); Oaks entry from a stable which has a fine record in this race and houses the current Oaks favourite, so market strength would probably be telling.
runners.comment: By Kingman out of Oaks winner Anapurna; won debut and second of ten in 1m Listed race here

runners.name: Lilt
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega half-sister to winners Doom (7f/1m including Listed; RPR 104) and Kilt (1m; 97); dam a well-related 1m-1m2f winner (including US Grade 1; 116); 7-2 for a six-runner maiden at Doncaster (7f, soft) in October, dictating a steady pace and battling on well to win by a neck; that's just fair form but this 1m Coronation Stakes and 1m4f Irish Oaks entry looks the type to do a lot better as 3yo.
runners.comment: 7f Doncaster maiden win as 2yo is just fair form but she looks the type to do a lot better

runners.name: Brigid's Well
runners.spotlight: Made all in one 1m novice as a 2yo and very nearly did so in another; 11-1 fourth of ten in Listed race here (1m, good to soft; Sacred Ground was second) on final start illustrated some limitations, sticking to her task well as if this longer trip would suit but, despite the Oaks entry, she probably has serious work to do in this company too.
runners.comment: 11-1 fourth of ten in 1m Listed race here on final 2yo start, with Sacred Ground in second

runners.name: Spinning Lizzie
runners.spotlight: Won debut as 2yo but twice came up short in Group events; resumed with a late charge to grab a conditions stakes at Kempton (1m, AW) in March but the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good; 22-1, made most) proved too tough and, despite a new trip, the same is likely to be the case today.
runners.comment: Has come up short in her three Group events and the same is likely to be the case today

runners.name: Maldives
runners.spotlight: 2,900,000gns yearling by Camelot; first foal of Group-placed 7f 2yo winner (RPR 102), herself a half-sister to multiple Group 1 winner Luxembourg; 8-1 in steadily run novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) three weeks ago, short of room for a sustained period before running on into second of seven; Irish Oaks entry who is bottom on ratings but probably capable of a lot better.
runners.comment: 2,900,000gns yearling by Camelot; hampered when second in novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good)

races.race_name: Fitzdares Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Ziggy Starshine
runners.spotlight: Landed quite a gamble when making a successful debut at Bath (5f, good; 15-8) 16 days ago, beating a subsequent winner with a lot more in hand than the neck margin would suggest; major player.
runners.comment: Won with more in hand than margin would suggest on Bath debut: form franked; major player

runners.name: Passerine
runners.spotlight: Sent off 7-1 when beaten just over 4l into fourth of 13 behind Ziggy Starshine at Bath (5f, good) 16 days ago; 5lb better off and open to improvement, but it will be needed.
runners.comment: Fourth behind Ziggy Starshine on Bath debut; open to improvement but it will be needed

runners.name: Babzini
runners.spotlight: March foal by St Mark's Basilica; 100,000gns yearling; dam 7.6f winner (RPR 82), sister to UAE 1m1f Group 2 winner Valiant Prince, half-sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Klassique, out of 1m Group 1 winner; stable can get them ready first time, but she may appreciate further in due course.
runners.comment: Stable can get them ready first time, but she may appreciate further in due course

runners.name: Nonrem
runners.spotlight: February foal by Oasis Dream; £50,000 yearling; closely related to 6f 2yo winner Apache Green (RPR 79); dam unraced half-sister to Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint winner Belvoir Bay, out of 1m winner; bred for speed, but stable's 2yo newcomers are usually better for a run.
runners.comment: Bred for speed, but stable's 2yo newcomers are usually better for a run

runners.name: Rhodes Runner
runners.spotlight: March foal by Minzaal; 75,000gns yearling; dam maiden half-sister to winners Sumiha (1m4f Group 3) and Suwayra (1m 2yo), out of Group-placed 1m2f winning half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner Sinawann; bred to stay further but the stable has made a bright start to the season with its 2yos; respected.
runners.comment: Bred to stay further but the stable has made a bright start to the season with its 2yos

runners.name: Lover Girl
runners.spotlight: Overcame the outside stall when making a winning debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW; 11-2) this month; the form has been franked, but this looks harder.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut at Wolverhampton; form has been franked, but this looks harder

runners.name: Survie
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare who has several pieces of Group 1 form over middle distances, including a Saudi effort since joining new yard this year; unsuited by drop back to 1m last time when one place behind Cathedral, losing her unbeaten AW record; big player on peak figures.
runners.comment: Has several pieces of Group 1 form, including Saudi effort for new yard; big player

runners.name: Cathedral
runners.spotlight: Possibly unsuited by soft ground; record of 0-8 since debut success doesn't do her justice, with three good efforts at Group/Grade 1 level (1m/1m3f) among her form; leading player on ratings returned to turf with AW reappearance under her belt.
runners.comment: Form includes three good efforts at Group/Grade 1 level; leading player on ratings

runners.name: Stateira
runners.spotlight: Doing well on AW and justified favouritism in 7f handicap on finals day, taking Newcastle record to 3-3; far from certain to sustain her progress over this longer trip back on turf (second attempt in this sphere).
runners.comment: Doing well on AW but is far from certain to sustain her progress back on turf

runners.name: Cheshire Dancer
runners.spotlight: Developed into a very useful sort last term, notably Group/Grade 3 performances at Ascot (won) and Kentucky Downs (ran well); chance of landing this prize depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of landing this prize depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: American Gal
runners.spotlight: Steady progress culminated in close second to Cheshire Dancer in Group 3 at Ascot (1m) on King George day last year; not in the same form since and this is a stiff task even if she rebounds.
runners.comment: Not in the same form since close second in Ascot Group 3 on King George day

runners.name: Sand Gazelle
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly; below form at Newmarket when last seen but broadly progressive otherwise and may have more to offer this term; Listed winner at York (1m2f) last July then ran well in Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f); shortlisted.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly; broadly progressive and may have more to offer this term

runners.name: Miss Justice
runners.spotlight: Form for the Gosdens featured a Listed win last August and close second in Newmarket Group 3 in October (both 1m2f); sold for 750,000gns since; this is harder but she may do better still.
runners.comment: Close second at Newmarket on final start for the Gosdens; sold 750,000gns since

runners.name: Arisaig
runners.spotlight: Ran well off top weight in handicap at the Newmarket Craven meeting (1m, good) but has something to find on ratings off level weights in this higher grade; not disgraced in Goodwood Listed event on Saturday.
runners.comment: Ran well in handicap at the Craven meeting; not disgraced at Goodwood on Saturday

runners.name: Falakeyah
runners.spotlight: Has proved headstrong and she was disappointing in last two appearances, including when only 5-2 for the Coronation Stakes having bypassed the Oaks and French Oaks, but this may be the time to catch her; comfortably justified favouritism in the Pretty Polly on this card (good to firm) 12 months ago, taking record to 2-2 and looking a potentially high-class filly; worth another chance.
runners.comment: Looks worth another chance to confirm the high promise of her win on this card last year

runners.name: Chantilly Lace
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; good fifth in the 1,000 Guineas and Coronation Stakes prior to close third in Ascot Group 3 won by Cheshire Dancer; below par in final 3yo start but consistent otherwise and may rebound.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; consistent until below par on last appearance; may rebound

runners.name: Francophone
runners.spotlight: Successful twice in Listed grade, including at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on last appearance; could go well but her overall record and ratings suggest she's unlikely to follow up in this better race.
runners.comment: Listed win at Newmarket on last appearance; unlikely to follow up in this grade

runners.name: Jancis
runners.spotlight: Irish mare whose career commenced with two Leopardstown wins, including in Group 3; 0-7 since but has continued to show useful form; unlikely to get back on the scoresheet in this field, however.
runners.comment: Useful sort but looks unlikely to regain the winning thread in this field

runners.name: Baileys Khelstar
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner; showed the benefit of his return from a long absence when giving it a good go from the front in a 1m6f handicap at Wolverhampton 16 days ago; whether he can improve again is debatable and this is tougher.
runners.comment: Second run back from an absence when second on the AW; this looks tougher
runners.owner: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Exposed 6yo who is 7-27 on AW surfaces but 1-12 on turf; respectable runs of late at Musselburgh but wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: Respectable runs of late at Musselburgh but wouldn't be the obvious answer

runners.name: Galyx
runners.spotlight: 1m/1m2f winner; has yet to entirely convince with his stamina beyond his last winning trip but that concern is clearly not shared by his trainer and he returns from an absence with the yard going well; any rain would be welcomed.
runners.comment: Has stamina to prove but returns with his yard going well; any rain welcomed

runners.name: Square Necker
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; his debut third in a 1m2f Doncaster maiden last March (good to soft) worked out exceptionally well and he then won easily at Dundalk in December (1m4f; odds-on; had been gelded), at the expense of a Listed-placed rival; could be on a useful mark for handicap debut and when second to Constitution Hill at Southwell (1m4f novice) he was giving the winner 5lb.
runners.comment: When second to Constitution Hill at Southwell (1m4f novice) he was giving the winner 5lb

runners.name: Mountain Road
runners.spotlight: 4-15 for David Simcock; absent for 582 days after a heavy defeat on debut for this yard and last week's comeback run at Musselburgh was just okay (2m, good); others preferred.
runners.comment: Last week's comeback run at Musselburgh was just okay (2m, good); others preferred

runners.name: Elysian Flame
runners.spotlight: Smart staying handicapper on the Flat for Mick and David Easterby in 2021 when competitive at some of the major meetings; having only his second run since that year when winning well over 2m at Beverley last month (good to soft); still useful, clearly, and has only gone up 2lb.
runners.comment: Second run since 2021 when winning well over 2m at Beverley last month; up 2lb

runners.name: Bahadur
runners.spotlight: Made immediate progress when switched to AW in the autumn, completing a hat-trick in a 1m4f novice and 1m4f/1m6f handicaps; perhaps in need of the race (albeit joint-favourite) on reappearance four weeks ago, but now needs to show that he can serve up his best on turf.
runners.comment: Made immediate progress when switched to AW in the autumn; needs to show show same on turf
runners.owner: J William Rowley & S P C Woods

runners.name: Goblet Of Fire
runners.spotlight: All of his Flat success for this yard has been on AW, albeit with good-ground hurdle wins too and he was runner-up at Goodwood (1m6f, good to firm) last August; has a run under his belt this term but to win this would require a new best performance on turf.
runners.comment: Has a run under his belt but to win this would require a new best performance on turf

runners.name: Dramatic Star
runners.spotlight: Improved last year without quite winning; each-way claims if back to best with first-time headgear after two rather disappointing runs in 2026.
runners.comment: E-w claims if back to best with first-time headgear after two rather disappointing runs

runners.name: Charging Thunder
runners.spotlight: Had underwhelming hurdle and AW campaigns before his second of five when returned to Flat turf at Beverley (2m) 18 days ago; this demands markedly better and his 2024 and 2025 turf records aren't that persuasive.
runners.comment: 2nd of 5 when returned to Flat turf at Beverley (2m) 18 days ago; this demands much more

runners.name: Yashin
runners.spotlight: Started 2025 with a bang for new connections when winning a slowly run, four-runner Group 3 at Ascot (2m, good to firm) in April; remote with ambitious targets on next three starts and although sixth of 11 in 2m handicap at Chester in September was an upturn, markedly better is required to win this..
runners.comment: Started 2025 with a bang (won slowly run Group 3) but there was only a whimper afterwards

runners.name: Brasil Power
runners.spotlight: Moving up to 1m4f worked well in December and comparable form at 1m6f/2m since, including another win three starts back; he's come up a bit short in warmer races this year, though, including behind Align The Stars at Kempton, and none of his turf form (admittedly at up to 1m2f) is close to this mark.
runners.comment: This race perhaps too tough; no turf form (admittedly at up to 1m2f) is close to this mark

runners.name: Pole Star
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term and two respectable efforts in valuable races in late summer; just 10-1 for 2m2f Cesarewitch at Newmarket on final 3yo outing, which did not work out, and he was just 15-8 when fading into fourth of six at Newbury (2m) 16 days ago on first run since he was gelded; connections now turn to a visor and drop him back in trip.
runners.comment: Just 15-8 when fading into 4th of six at Newbury (2m); visor and back down in trip
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Artisan Dancer
runners.spotlight: Industrious and dependable sort over 1m6f-2m2f, who's fit from AW, but his turf strike-rate is just 1-17 and that makes him look vulnerable.
runners.comment: Industrious and dependable sort over 1m6f-2m2f but his turf strike-rate is just 1-17

runners.name: Many Men
runners.spotlight: Excellent progress last term included three handicap wins (1m2f, 1m6f and 2m) in March-July and an agonisingly close call in the Melrose at York in August; close fourth in 1m6f Listed race at Ascot on final outing; well below that form on 2026 reappearance but that may have put him right.
runners.comment: Excellent progress as 3yo; well below best on 2026 return but that may have put him right
runners.owner: J Finch, Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

runners.name: Subsequent
runners.spotlight: Excellent 3yo season culminated in his Listed win at Ascot (1m6f, soft) and, after a delayed reappearance last year, he found career-best form to win a good handicap at Doncaster (14.5f, good to soft; tongue tied second time) in September on final outing; the Yorkshire Cup and Ascot Gold Cup entries are striking but ideally he would be on ground softer than good.
runners.comment: Yorkshire Cup and Ascot Gold Cup entries are striking but ground softer than good is ideal

runners.name: Align The Stars
runners.spotlight: Thereabouts on occasions in 2025, crept down the weights and broke his losing run in clearcut style when he reappeared five weeks ago wearing restored cheekpieces in the Queen's Prize at Kempton (2m, AW); back up 4lb but showed plenty of bottle in that win and may have turned a corner.
runners.comment: May have turned a corner when he broke his losing run in clearcut style at Kempton (AW)

runners.name: Kihavah
runners.spotlight: Saw daylight all too late when strong-finishing fifth in this race last year (much better than his 2024 performance in it); Meydan this January/February did not yield much (stiff tasks) but he's now 4lb lower than this time last year, too well handicapped to be ruled out; rare run in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Saw daylight all too late when strong-finishing 5th in this race last year off 4lb higher

runners.name: Precise
runners.spotlight: Excellent progress last term culminated in Group 1 wins at the Curragh (7f, yielding) and Newmarket, bringing up a four-timer; drew clear inside the final 1f and comfortably beat Venetian Lace by just over 3l in the Fillies' Mile over C&D (good) on the final occasion; had a minor setback to her schedule with a slightly raised temperature in March but is apparently fine now; trainer has landed this race seven times; leading contender.
runners.comment: Continued her excellent progress with comfortable win in the Fillies' Mile; leading player

runners.name: My Highness
runners.spotlight: Ended her 2yo campaign with quite a tidy success in Group 2 at Deauville (7f, good) to complete a hat-trick; beaten favourite (finished second, splitting outsiders) in the Prix Imprudence over the same C&D on reappearance but that was also the case with the same yard's Miss France, who rebounded to land this prize in 2014; likely to be suited by stepping up to 1m; enters calculations, with a resumption of improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Similar profile to the same yard's winner of this race in 2014; may well resume progress

runners.name: True Love
runners.spotlight: In the same ball park as stablemate Precise on ratings and, like that filly, is a Group 1 winner at Newmarket (Cheveley Park Stakes over 6f); landed the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot (5f) earlier in her 2yo campaign and added a Group 3 prize, with something to spare, at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; has a solid record aside from an excusable defeat at the Breeders' Cup; upped further in trip and has stamina on the dam's side; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Cheveley Park Stakes winner; has a largely solid record; one of the main contenders
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Venetian Sun
runners.spotlight: Absent since finishing third to Precise in the Moyglare Stud Stakes when upped to 7f at the Curragh, losing her unbeaten record but far from disgraced and sticking on in a manner that suggests she'll stay 1m this year; 4-4 previously and completed a notable 6f hat-trick with wins in Group 3 at Royal Ascot, Group 2 at Newmarket (July) and Group 1 at Deauville; may well rate higher still and add to an already impressive tally; pleased her trainer in a racecourse gallop last month; highly respected.
runners.comment: Below-form 3rd to Precise on final 2yo effort but 4-4 previously; may well resume progress

runners.name: Evolutionist
runners.spotlight: Newmarket form comprises a maiden win on the July course and third-place finish, just over 4l behind Precise, in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile; bagged Group 3 honours at Longchamp (1m, good) on reappearance, collaring a sitting duck (clear leader who tired) and strong at the finish; has evidently trained on well and her RPRs continue on an upward trajectory.
runners.comment: Strong at the finish in Group 3 at Longchamp, showing that she has trained on well

runners.name: Azleet
runners.spotlight: Belied her 50-1 odds with neck success in the Nell Gwyn (upped to 7f, good; Group 3) on seasonal debut, taking strike-rate to 3-8 and form figures on the Newmarket tracks to 121; doesn't look a budding star and only two fillies in the last 40 years have landed that trial then followed up in the 1,000 Guineas; may well come up short.
runners.comment: Won the Nell Gwyn but doesn't look a budding star and may well come up short

runners.name: Abashiri
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly, by Frankel out of a 1m2f Group 1 scorer for her connections; quickened smartly and recorded an impressive 4l success in Kempton maiden (1m, AW) six months ago; the shortest on experience, being the sole once-raced contender, but brings major potential; interesting.
runners.comment: Well bred; quickened smartly for an impressive win at Kempton; brings major potential

runners.name: Mubasimah
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly who has been campaigned exclusively over 7f at Newmarket, producing good performances (won maiden and second in Group 3) from the front last autumn and then not asked to lead when only sixth, beaten favourite, in the Nell Gwyn; may still have more to offer but this is a difficult task in terms of form and tactical considerations.
runners.comment: Only sixth when favourite for the Nell Gwyn; low in calculations after that effort

runners.name: Timeforshowcasing
runners.spotlight: Held on gamely in 1m Listed event at Newcastle on seasonal debut, taking AW record to 2-2 and overall strike-rate to 4-6; may win more races at the right level but looks out of her depth in this company returned to turf, as her two defeats (both Group 2) attest.
runners.comment: Has built up a good-strike rate but looks out of her depth at this level

runners.name: Darn Hot Gallop
runners.spotlight: From the family of Enable; edged ahead close home in C&D handicap (good; ran off a mark of 83) at the Craven meeting, adding to her 2yo AW wins; it's hard to knock an improving filly who remains unbeaten (3-3) but her form is a long way short of Classic standard, as BHA ratings reflect.
runners.comment: Won C&D handicap at the Craven meeting, taking record to 3-3; this is much harder

runners.name: Inis Mor
runners.spotlight: Solid 2yo form with two novice wins on the Newmarket July course and a good second in valuable conditions race at Longchamp (sole 1m attempt); didn't particularly boost her Guineas claims with 2l fourth to Asleet in the Nell Gwyn (7f, good) but she may take a step forward with that reappearance outing under her belt.
runners.comment: Solid 2yo form; may take a step forward with Nell Gwyn reappearance under her belt

runners.name: Touleen
runners.spotlight: Gained her 2yo wins in stylish fashion and made a satisfactory reappearance as favourite for the Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) when a close second under Saffie Osborne; bit to prove back at Newmarket, having looked ill at ease here in the Rockfel, but a plausible danger provided she handles the track better this time; from the family of 1,000 Guineas winner Ghanaati and retains potential.
runners.comment: Satisfactory reappearance in the Fred Darling; from the family of a 1,000 Guineas winner

runners.name: The Prettiest Star
runners.spotlight: Daughter of her connections' July Cup winner Starman; duly showed plenty of promise in both 2yo starts, recording a clearcut success at Nottingham (6f, good) and close second, clear of remainder, in the Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm; Group 2); gives the impression she'll be suited by this further step up in distance; possibilities with additional improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Clear second in the Rockfel; possibilities with further improvement on the cards

runners.name: Domina Ignis
runners.spotlight: Very similar profile to the 2024 Guineas winner Elmalka, having won at Southwell in sole 2yo start then finished third in the Fred Darling (Group 3); gained her success (6f, AW) in emphatic fashion despite taking a good hold, then was beaten only 2l with hood fitted at Newbury (7f, good) despite being hampered; bred to stay 1m and looks open to further progress.
runners.comment: Good third in the Fred Darling; very similar profile to Elmalka, who won this race in 2024

runners.name: Spicy Marg
runners.spotlight: Proved a useful sprinter last season, notably winning over 5f at Glorious Goodwood in the summer and over 6f at Newmarket (beat Azleet in good-ground Listed contest) final outing; not an obvious 1,000 Guineas player, with doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters (she's a stone below the standard required).
runners.comment: Useful sprinter last season; has doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters

runners.name: Silenciosa
runners.spotlight: Scored with something to spare at Southwell (AW) in sole 2yo start, then did well to finish sixth in the Fred Darling at Newbury (again over 7f) having been slowly away, met traffic issues and been forced to switch; remains a nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands another big leap forward on bare figures.
runners.comment: Nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands plenty of further progress

runners.name: Venetian Lace
runners.spotlight: Belied market position (rank outsider) by finishing second to Precise in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile over C&D but needs to prove she can back up that apparent improvement; may be flattered by her new rating and, combined with a record of 0-5 since debut success, doesn't look a solid contestant.
runners.comment: Needs to prove she can back up the form of her big-priced second in the Fillies' Mile
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Rose Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Has something to find at this level but is open to further improvement and would be particularly interesting if the ground is on the soft side, having shown her 2yo promise on slower than good, namely a narrow success in newcomers' event at Deauville (7f) and a rallying fourth in the May Hill at Doncaster (1m Group 2).
runners.comment: Showed her 2yo promise on softer than good; interesting if the ground is similar

runners.name: True Test
runners.spotlight: Career commenced with two wins, the second one on the Newmarket July course; record of 0-5 since reflects that her limitations are rather exposed, and this is a markedly stronger Guineas than the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas in which she went close at Meydan (1m, good; Listed) last time.
runners.comment: Went close in the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas at Meydan; this is a much stronger race

races.race_name: Queen Mother Memorial Cup (Group 3 Handicap) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Nautical Force
runners.spotlight: Useful at up to 1m4f when when trained in Ireland and came from off the pace to make winning Hong Kong debut here (1m1f) last month; back up in trip today and needs to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Scored over 1m1f on recent Hong Kong debut and this longer trip will suit

runners.name: Stunning Peach
runners.spotlight: Took form to new level when winning twice in February/March (1m/1m1f); subsequent Group 2 defeat is not viewed too harshly but he was only ninth behind Nautical Force when back in a handicap (1m1f) two weeks ago; needs to kick on again over this new trip.
runners.comment: Dual winner this year but only ninth behind Nautical Force here last month

runners.name: Winning Wing
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner over 1m2f this season (unraced over further); ran about as well as could have been expected when third of six (no match for winner) in Group 1 over that trip in March but could only manage eighth behind Nautical Force when dropped back to 1m1f two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Triple 1m2f course winner this season; safely held when dropped back to 1m1f last time

runners.name: Romantic Thor
runners.spotlight: In good form over 1m on dirt in recent months, making the frame on his last three appearances; should be fine back on turf and stays at least 1m2f but is 6lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: In good form on dirt lately; should be fine back on turf but is 6lb wrong
runners.owner: Peter Lau Pak Fai & Suki Tang Xianfang

runners.name: Fortune Boy
runners.spotlight: Made the frame three consecutive times in February/March (1m1f-1m2f) but was a shade disappointing when last in action and competes from 4lb out of the weights today; needs to find something extra over the new trip.
runners.comment: Largely in good form this season but competes from 4lb out of the weights today
runners.owner: Ricky Wong Kin Chat & Helen Leung Yee Wan

runners.name: Joy Of Spring
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over 1m1f; placed on last two appearances, more recently when third over that trip behind Nautical Force here a fortnight ago; has another big role to play if this extra 3f suits.
runners.comment: Ran well when third over 1m1f here last month; goes beyond that trip for first time
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung

runners.name: Gentlemen Legacy
runners.spotlight: Improving 5yo who won consecutive 1m2f races here in February/March and again ran well when fourth in 1m1f contest won by Nautical Force a fortnight ago; tries a new trip today; respected.
runners.comment: Dual 1m2f winner this year and again ran well over 1m1f last month; respected

runners.name: West Wind Blows
runners.spotlight: Two-time Group 3 winner here over shorter, who has only finished outside the first three four times and heads into this having matched his career-best behind the mighty Calandagan in March in the Group 1 Sheema Classic (1m4f, good); leading claims here significantly down in grade.
runners.comment: Matched his career-best last time and hard to beat in that sort of form

runners.name: Map Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Group 2 winner and Group 1-placed over 1m2f earlier in his career at this track but hasn't really built on that since, including over this trip the last two times having been gelded in between; not easy to recommend on that basis but owner's retained rider seemingly prefers him to Best Secret, so a return to form couldn't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Not at best last two times but James Doyle rides him instead of Best Secret

runners.name: Oracle
runners.spotlight: Supplemented for this; another lightly raced sort in the field, who holds the distinction of being odds on to beat top Group 1 performer Daryz as a 3yo in a conditions event here; ran okay on only previous try at this trip and improved on the figures last time when fifth, beaten only 1.5l and ahead of Best Secret, so couldn't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Supplemented for this; good run last time and may do better still

runners.name: Best Secret
runners.spotlight: Not had much racing for his age and only been over this trip once before, but that was a good effort in beating the well-regarded Gethin in a Listed event; arrives unexposed over 1m4f and not too far off his best on his recent return to action over 1m2f under this rider on first venture into Group company, but the owner's retained jockey appears to have chosen Map Of Stars.
runners.comment: Unexposed at the trip and ran well last time, but Doyle prefers Map Of Stars seemingly

runners.name: Chestnut Rocket
runners.spotlight: Smart performer at his best and was just below his peak a month and a half ago in a 1m4f Listed event at Saint-Cloud on very soft; should be okay on quicker ground and fully respected, but this is a strong race for the grade and he'll need extra.
runners.comment: Not far off his best last time so respected for all that more is needed

runners.name: Euboa
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form, with trip and ground likely to suit, but hasn't won a Listed race yet let alone a Group race, which suggests she has plenty on even with the 4lb sex allowance; hard task.
runners.comment: Hasn't won a Listed race yet let alone a Group race; hard task

races.race_name: Oliver Brown Pretty Polly Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Esna
runners.spotlight: All three starts as 2yo were on soft; green in front in 7f novice win at Sandown against a notable rival; about 2l fourth of eight when an outsider for 1m Group 1 Marcel Boussac at Longchamp, keeping on well while never able to threaten seriously the lead; fair chance she'll stay 1m2f, judged on that, even if pedigree (sire sprinter) casts doubts on her Oaks entry; clear top on form.
runners.comment: Clear top on form after her Group 1 Marcel Boussac fourth; fair chance she'll stay 1m2f

runners.name: Jennifer Jane
runners.spotlight: Made all in a maiden at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on second start and almost all in Listed race at Sandown (7f, good) later last summer; her season ended, however, with last of seven in Group 3 at Goodwood (7f, good to firm) in August; good chance she will stay 1m2f; needs to resume progress.
runners.comment: Good chance she will stay 1m2f; needs to resume progress after last in a Group 3 in August

runners.name: Sacred Ground
runners.spotlight: By Kingman out of Oaks winner Anapurna; favourite for both starts as 2yo, overcoming greenness to win a five-runner novice at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) and every chance through the final 3f when second of ten in Listed race at Newmarket (1m, good to soft); Oaks entry from a stable which has a fine record in this race and houses the current Oaks favourite, so market strength would probably be telling.
runners.comment: By Kingman out of Oaks winner Anapurna; won debut and second of ten in 1m Listed race here

runners.name: Lilt
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega half-sister to winners Doom (7f/1m including Listed; RPR 104) and Kilt (1m; 97); dam a well-related 1m-1m2f winner (including US Grade 1; 116); 7-2 for a six-runner maiden at Doncaster (7f, soft) in October, dictating a steady pace and battling on well to win by a neck; that's just fair form but this 1m Coronation Stakes and 1m4f Irish Oaks entry looks the type to do a lot better as 3yo.
runners.comment: 7f Doncaster maiden win as 2yo is just fair form but she looks the type to do a lot better

runners.name: Brigid's Well
runners.spotlight: Made all in one 1m novice as a 2yo and very nearly did so in another; 11-1 fourth of ten in Listed race here (1m, good to soft; Sacred Ground was second) on final start illustrated some limitations, sticking to her task well as if this longer trip would suit but, despite the Oaks entry, she probably has serious work to do in this company too.
runners.comment: 11-1 fourth of ten in 1m Listed race here on final 2yo start, with Sacred Ground in second

runners.name: Spinning Lizzie
runners.spotlight: Won debut as 2yo but twice came up short in Group events; resumed with a late charge to grab a conditions stakes at Kempton (1m, AW) in March but the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good; 22-1, made most) proved too tough and, despite a new trip, the same is likely to be the case today.
runners.comment: Has come up short in her three Group events and the same is likely to be the case today

runners.name: Maldives
runners.spotlight: 2,900,000gns yearling by Camelot; first foal of Group-placed 7f 2yo winner (RPR 102), herself a half-sister to multiple Group 1 winner Luxembourg; 8-1 in steadily run novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) three weeks ago, short of room for a sustained period before running on into second of seven; Irish Oaks entry who is bottom on ratings but probably capable of a lot better.
runners.comment: 2,900,000gns yearling by Camelot; hampered when second in novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good)

runners.name: Survie
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare who has several pieces of Group 1 form over middle distances, including a Saudi effort since joining new yard this year; unsuited by drop back to 1m last time when one place behind Cathedral, losing her unbeaten AW record; big player on peak figures.
runners.comment: Has several pieces of Group 1 form, including Saudi effort for new yard; big player

runners.name: Cathedral
runners.spotlight: Possibly unsuited by soft ground; record of 0-8 since debut success doesn't do her justice, with three good efforts at Group/Grade 1 level (1m/1m3f) among her form; leading player on ratings returned to turf with AW reappearance under her belt.
runners.comment: Form includes three good efforts at Group/Grade 1 level; leading player on ratings

runners.name: Stateira
runners.spotlight: Doing well on AW and justified favouritism in 7f handicap on finals day, taking Newcastle record to 3-3; far from certain to sustain her progress over this longer trip back on turf (second attempt in this sphere).
runners.comment: Doing well on AW but is far from certain to sustain her progress back on turf

runners.name: Cheshire Dancer
runners.spotlight: Developed into a very useful sort last term, notably Group/Grade 3 performances at Ascot (won) and Kentucky Downs (ran well); chance of landing this prize depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of landing this prize depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: American Gal
runners.spotlight: Steady progress culminated in close second to Cheshire Dancer in Group 3 at Ascot (1m) on King George day last year; not in the same form since and this is a stiff task even if she rebounds.
runners.comment: Not in the same form since close second in Ascot Group 3 on King George day

runners.name: Sand Gazelle
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly; below form at Newmarket when last seen but broadly progressive otherwise and may have more to offer this term; Listed winner at York (1m2f) last July then ran well in Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f); shortlisted.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly; broadly progressive and may have more to offer this term

runners.name: Miss Justice
runners.spotlight: Form for the Gosdens featured a Listed win last August and close second in Newmarket Group 3 in October (both 1m2f); sold for 750,000gns since; this is harder but she may do better still.
runners.comment: Close second at Newmarket on final start for the Gosdens; sold 750,000gns since

runners.name: Arisaig
runners.spotlight: Ran well off top weight in handicap at the Newmarket Craven meeting (1m, good) but has something to find on ratings off level weights in this higher grade; not disgraced in Goodwood Listed event on Saturday.
runners.comment: Ran well in handicap at the Craven meeting; not disgraced at Goodwood on Saturday

runners.name: Falakeyah
runners.spotlight: Has proved headstrong and she was disappointing in last two appearances, including when only 5-2 for the Coronation Stakes having bypassed the Oaks and French Oaks, but this may be the time to catch her; comfortably justified favouritism in the Pretty Polly on this card (good to firm) 12 months ago, taking record to 2-2 and looking a potentially high-class filly; worth another chance.
runners.comment: Looks worth another chance to confirm the high promise of her win on this card last year

runners.name: Chantilly Lace
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; good fifth in the 1,000 Guineas and Coronation Stakes prior to close third in Ascot Group 3 won by Cheshire Dancer; below par in final 3yo start but consistent otherwise and may rebound.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; consistent until below par on last appearance; may rebound

runners.name: Francophone
runners.spotlight: Successful twice in Listed grade, including at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on last appearance; could go well but her overall record and ratings suggest she's unlikely to follow up in this better race.
runners.comment: Listed win at Newmarket on last appearance; unlikely to follow up in this grade

runners.name: Jancis
runners.spotlight: Irish mare whose career commenced with two Leopardstown wins, including in Group 3; 0-7 since but has continued to show useful form; unlikely to get back on the scoresheet in this field, however.
runners.comment: Useful sort but looks unlikely to regain the winning thread in this field

races.race_name: Goffs Irish EBF Polonia Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Havana Anna
runners.spotlight: Maiden and Listed winner over 5f; beaten only a nose in a Group 3 over the minimum trip at Longchamp, though produced her best effort when chasing home True Love in the 6f Group 1 Cheveley Park; failed to cope with the challenge of the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; the clear pick on overall form.
runners.comment: Listed winner/Group 3 second, second in Cheveley Park, can be forgiven Breeders' Cup run

runners.name: Ipanema Queen
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Curragh maiden on debut but seemed better suited to 5f as the season progressed; won two Listed races at the Curragh and Dundalk over the minimum trip after finishing 3 3/4l second to Havana Anna at Naas; lost out narrowly to a longer-priced stablemate at Dundalk on seasonal debut; should reach the money.
runners.comment: No match for Havana Anna at Naas, has solid Listed-winning form, went close on AW in March
runners.owner: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Thenandnow
runners.spotlight: Officially rated 75 after four runs at two, the best of which was a 1 3/4l second of eight on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good-to-firm); now promoted to 85 after winning a 24-runner maiden at the Curragh; that was not a strong race, and she faces a tough task now.
runners.comment: Won a 24-runner maiden at the Curragh, needs a lot more now judged on her official rating

runners.name: Royal Bay Cen
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner in France at two; posted her two best efforts in defeat in a 7f Group 3 at Longchamp and a 6f Group 3 at Chantilly; has never run over 5f, but her dam Royal Razalma won the Cornwallis.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner in France at two; posted her two best efforts in defeat over further

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Has run 11 times, with two of her three wins gained over C&D; failed to progress from a maiden win last May; sold for 9,000GBP in January and got back to form in winning a 5f Naas handicap on stable debut; fifth in a 6f handicap at the Curragh early here last month; reappeared over C&D the following day to win a 5f conditions race; more on her plate.
runners.comment: Lost form after C&D win last May, has got her career back on track with two wins this term

runners.name: Quiet Mutiny
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 6f winner from the family of outstanding sprinter Lochsong; justified good support on debut in a 6f maiden at Fairyhouse last July; the form could hardly have worked out better, runner-up Precise won her next four races, completing a Group 1 double in the Moyglare and Fillies' Mile.
runners.comment: Beat subsequent dual Group 1 winner Precise over 6f on only start at two, smart prospect

runners.name: Rosie Frith
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden winner last July on the fourth of eight stats at two; beaten a nose in an auction series race over 6f at this venue before going down by a neck in a 5f Dundalk event; signed off with a nursery win at Dundalk; struggled on her only run in a stakes race.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, solid form later but struggled on her only run in a stakes race

runners.name: Tornado Kiss
runners.spotlight: Narrowly beaten twice in Naas fillies' maidens before landing the odds in a seven-runner C&D maiden last September; outpaced in the closing stages in a 5f Listed race at Dundalk in October; not suited by the race conditions, but stable in good form.
runners.comment: Outpaced in the closing stages in a 5f Listed race at Dundalk last October, tough task

runners.name: Dawn Romance
runners.spotlight: Beaten 6l by Havana Anna over 5f at Naas on her debut; won a 6f maiden at the same venue on good ground on her third start; in rear in a 6f Listed race at Fairyhouse in September.
runners.comment: No match for Havana Anna on debut, 6f Naas winner, in rear in a Listed race at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Bear On The Run
runners.spotlight: Started with a respectable fifth of 14 at the Curragh last May; made all to win over the same trip at Dundalk on August on her only other start at two; half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Dun Na Sead, worth a shot at this.
runners.comment: Dundalk 6f winner, half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Dun Na Sead, worth a shot at this
runners.owner: Kildare Racing Syndicate & Kilmichael Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Precise
runners.spotlight: Excellent progress last term culminated in Group 1 wins at the Curragh (7f, yielding) and Newmarket, bringing up a four-timer; drew clear inside the final 1f and comfortably beat Venetian Lace by just over 3l in the Fillies' Mile over C&D (good) on the final occasion; had a minor setback to her schedule with a slightly raised temperature in March but is apparently fine now; trainer has landed this race seven times; leading contender.
runners.comment: Continued her excellent progress with comfortable win in the Fillies' Mile; leading player

runners.name: My Highness
runners.spotlight: Ended her 2yo campaign with quite a tidy success in Group 2 at Deauville (7f, good) to complete a hat-trick; beaten favourite (finished second, splitting outsiders) in the Prix Imprudence over the same C&D on reappearance but that was also the case with the same yard's Miss France, who rebounded to land this prize in 2014; likely to be suited by stepping up to 1m; enters calculations, with a resumption of improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Similar profile to the same yard's winner of this race in 2014; may well resume progress

runners.name: True Love
runners.spotlight: In the same ball park as stablemate Precise on ratings and, like that filly, is a Group 1 winner at Newmarket (Cheveley Park Stakes over 6f); landed the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot (5f) earlier in her 2yo campaign and added a Group 3 prize, with something to spare, at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; has a solid record aside from an excusable defeat at the Breeders' Cup; upped further in trip and has stamina on the dam's side; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Cheveley Park Stakes winner; has a largely solid record; one of the main contenders
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Venetian Sun
runners.spotlight: Absent since finishing third to Precise in the Moyglare Stud Stakes when upped to 7f at the Curragh, losing her unbeaten record but far from disgraced and sticking on in a manner that suggests she'll stay 1m this year; 4-4 previously and completed a notable 6f hat-trick with wins in Group 3 at Royal Ascot, Group 2 at Newmarket (July) and Group 1 at Deauville; may well rate higher still and add to an already impressive tally; pleased her trainer in a racecourse gallop last month; highly respected.
runners.comment: Below-form 3rd to Precise on final 2yo effort but 4-4 previously; may well resume progress

runners.name: Evolutionist
runners.spotlight: Newmarket form comprises a maiden win on the July course and third-place finish, just over 4l behind Precise, in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile; bagged Group 3 honours at Longchamp (1m, good) on reappearance, collaring a sitting duck (clear leader who tired) and strong at the finish; has evidently trained on well and her RPRs continue on an upward trajectory.
runners.comment: Strong at the finish in Group 3 at Longchamp, showing that she has trained on well

runners.name: Azleet
runners.spotlight: Belied her 50-1 odds with neck success in the Nell Gwyn (upped to 7f, good; Group 3) on seasonal debut, taking strike-rate to 3-8 and form figures on the Newmarket tracks to 121; doesn't look a budding star and only two fillies in the last 40 years have landed that trial then followed up in the 1,000 Guineas; may well come up short.
runners.comment: Won the Nell Gwyn but doesn't look a budding star and may well come up short

runners.name: Abashiri
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly, by Frankel out of a 1m2f Group 1 scorer for her connections; quickened smartly and recorded an impressive 4l success in Kempton maiden (1m, AW) six months ago; the shortest on experience, being the sole once-raced contender, but brings major potential; interesting.
runners.comment: Well bred; quickened smartly for an impressive win at Kempton; brings major potential

runners.name: Mubasimah
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly who has been campaigned exclusively over 7f at Newmarket, producing good performances (won maiden and second in Group 3) from the front last autumn and then not asked to lead when only sixth, beaten favourite, in the Nell Gwyn; may still have more to offer but this is a difficult task in terms of form and tactical considerations.
runners.comment: Only sixth when favourite for the Nell Gwyn; low in calculations after that effort

runners.name: Timeforshowcasing
runners.spotlight: Held on gamely in 1m Listed event at Newcastle on seasonal debut, taking AW record to 2-2 and overall strike-rate to 4-6; may win more races at the right level but looks out of her depth in this company returned to turf, as her two defeats (both Group 2) attest.
runners.comment: Has built up a good-strike rate but looks out of her depth at this level

runners.name: Darn Hot Gallop
runners.spotlight: From the family of Enable; edged ahead close home in C&D handicap (good; ran off a mark of 83) at the Craven meeting, adding to her 2yo AW wins; it's hard to knock an improving filly who remains unbeaten (3-3) but her form is a long way short of Classic standard, as BHA ratings reflect.
runners.comment: Won C&D handicap at the Craven meeting, taking record to 3-3; this is much harder

runners.name: Inis Mor
runners.spotlight: Solid 2yo form with two novice wins on the Newmarket July course and a good second in valuable conditions race at Longchamp (sole 1m attempt); didn't particularly boost her Guineas claims with 2l fourth to Asleet in the Nell Gwyn (7f, good) but she may take a step forward with that reappearance outing under her belt.
runners.comment: Solid 2yo form; may take a step forward with Nell Gwyn reappearance under her belt

runners.name: Touleen
runners.spotlight: Gained her 2yo wins in stylish fashion and made a satisfactory reappearance as favourite for the Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) when a close second under Saffie Osborne; bit to prove back at Newmarket, having looked ill at ease here in the Rockfel, but a plausible danger provided she handles the track better this time; from the family of 1,000 Guineas winner Ghanaati and retains potential.
runners.comment: Satisfactory reappearance in the Fred Darling; from the family of a 1,000 Guineas winner

runners.name: The Prettiest Star
runners.spotlight: Daughter of her connections' July Cup winner Starman; duly showed plenty of promise in both 2yo starts, recording a clearcut success at Nottingham (6f, good) and close second, clear of remainder, in the Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm; Group 2); gives the impression she'll be suited by this further step up in distance; possibilities with additional improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Clear second in the Rockfel; possibilities with further improvement on the cards

runners.name: Domina Ignis
runners.spotlight: Very similar profile to the 2024 Guineas winner Elmalka, having won at Southwell in sole 2yo start then finished third in the Fred Darling (Group 3); gained her success (6f, AW) in emphatic fashion despite taking a good hold, then was beaten only 2l with hood fitted at Newbury (7f, good) despite being hampered; bred to stay 1m and looks open to further progress.
runners.comment: Good third in the Fred Darling; very similar profile to Elmalka, who won this race in 2024

runners.name: Spicy Marg
runners.spotlight: Proved a useful sprinter last season, notably winning over 5f at Glorious Goodwood in the summer and over 6f at Newmarket (beat Azleet in good-ground Listed contest) final outing; not an obvious 1,000 Guineas player, with doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters (she's a stone below the standard required).
runners.comment: Useful sprinter last season; has doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters

runners.name: Silenciosa
runners.spotlight: Scored with something to spare at Southwell (AW) in sole 2yo start, then did well to finish sixth in the Fred Darling at Newbury (again over 7f) having been slowly away, met traffic issues and been forced to switch; remains a nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands another big leap forward on bare figures.
runners.comment: Nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands plenty of further progress

runners.name: Venetian Lace
runners.spotlight: Belied market position (rank outsider) by finishing second to Precise in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile over C&D but needs to prove she can back up that apparent improvement; may be flattered by her new rating and, combined with a record of 0-5 since debut success, doesn't look a solid contestant.
runners.comment: Needs to prove she can back up the form of her big-priced second in the Fillies' Mile
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Rose Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Has something to find at this level but is open to further improvement and would be particularly interesting if the ground is on the soft side, having shown her 2yo promise on slower than good, namely a narrow success in newcomers' event at Deauville (7f) and a rallying fourth in the May Hill at Doncaster (1m Group 2).
runners.comment: Showed her 2yo promise on softer than good; interesting if the ground is similar

runners.name: True Test
runners.spotlight: Career commenced with two wins, the second one on the Newmarket July course; record of 0-5 since reflects that her limitations are rather exposed, and this is a markedly stronger Guineas than the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas in which she went close at Meydan (1m, good; Listed) last time.
runners.comment: Went close in the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas at Meydan; this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Precise
runners.spotlight: Excellent progress last term culminated in Group 1 wins at the Curragh (7f, yielding) and Newmarket, bringing up a four-timer; drew clear inside the final 1f and comfortably beat Venetian Lace by just over 3l in the Fillies' Mile over C&D (good) on the final occasion; had a minor setback to her schedule with a slightly raised temperature in March but is apparently fine now; trainer has landed this race seven times; leading contender.

runners.comment: Solid 2yo form; may take a step forward with Nell Gwyn reappearance under her belt

runners.name: Touleen
runners.spotlight: Gained her 2yo wins in stylish fashion and made a satisfactory reappearance as favourite for the Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) when a close second under Saffie Osborne; bit to prove back at Newmarket, having looked ill at ease here in the Rockfel, but a plausible danger provided she handles the track better this time; from the family of 1,000 Guineas winner Ghanaati and retains potential.
runners.comment: Satisfactory reappearance in the Fred Darling; from the family of a 1,000 Guineas winner

runners.name: The Prettiest Star
runners.spotlight: Daughter of her connections' July Cup winner Starman; duly showed plenty of promise in both 2yo starts, recording a clearcut success at Nottingham (6f, good) and close second, clear of remainder, in the Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm; Group 2); gives the impression she'll be suited by this further step up in distance; possibilities with additional improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Clear second in the Rockfel; possibilities with further improvement on the cards

runners.name: Domina Ignis
runners.spotlight: Very similar profile to the 2024 Guineas winner Elmalka, having won at Southwell in sole 2yo start then finished third in the Fred Darling (Group 3); gained her success (6f, AW) in emphatic fashion despite taking a good hold, then was beaten only 2l with hood fitted at Newbury (7f, good) despite being hampered; bred to stay 1m and looks open to further progress.
runners.comment: Good third in the Fred Darling; very similar profile to Elmalka, who won this race in 2024

runners.name: Spicy Marg
runners.spotlight: Proved a useful sprinter last season, notably winning over 5f at Glorious Goodwood in the summer and over 6f at Newmarket (beat Azleet in good-ground Listed contest) final outing; not an obvious 1,000 Guineas player, with doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters (she's a stone below the standard required).
runners.comment: Useful sprinter last season; has doubts over the new trip and the deeper waters

runners.name: Silenciosa
runners.spotlight: Scored with something to spare at Southwell (AW) in sole 2yo start, then did well to finish sixth in the Fred Darling at Newbury (again over 7f) having been slowly away, met traffic issues and been forced to switch; remains a nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands another big leap forward on bare figures.
runners.comment: Nice prospect for Tom Clover but this assignment demands plenty of further progress

runners.name: Venetian Lace
runners.spotlight: Belied market position (rank outsider) by finishing second to Precise in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile over C&D but needs to prove she can back up that apparent improvement; may be flattered by her new rating and, combined with a record of 0-5 since debut success, doesn't look a solid contestant.
runners.comment: Needs to prove she can back up the form of her big-priced second in the Fillies' Mile
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Rose Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Has something to find at this level but is open to further improvement and would be particularly interesting if the ground is on the soft side, having shown her 2yo promise on slower than good, namely a narrow success in newcomers' event at Deauville (7f) and a rallying fourth in the May Hill at Doncaster (1m Group 2).
runners.comment: Showed her 2yo promise on softer than good; interesting if the ground is similar

runners.name: True Test
runners.spotlight: Career commenced with two wins, the second one on the Newmarket July course; record of 0-5 since reflects that her limitations are rather exposed, and this is a markedly stronger Guineas than the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas in which she went close at Meydan (1m, good; Listed) last time.
runners.comment: Went close in the Jumeirah 1,000 Guineas at Meydan; this is a much stronger race
